items.title: Court Of Appeal, April 30, 2026, Order Rejecting Appeal As Inadmissible, UPC_CoA_1/2026
items.title_zh: UPC上诉法院裁定驳回上诉：程序合规与费用担保标准
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Rejects Adobe Appeal: Strict Procedural Compliance and Cost Security Standards
items.summary: The Court of Appeal verifies ex officio compliance with the time limits and rules governing the appeal. When deciding on the admissibility of an appeal, an order rejecting the appeal as...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉法院在Adobe诉Keeex案中，裁定驳回上诉并确认程序规则需严格适用以保障法律确定性。法院依职权审查上诉期限，认定驳回裁定未超出请求范围。此外，法院重申原告与被告的相对财务状况不构成Rule 158下的费用担保考量因素。该判决强化了UPC程序规则的刚性，强调避免司法任意性，对后续涉及费用担保及程序合规的案件具有先例约束力。
items.ai_summary_en: In the case of Adobe Inc. v. Keeex SAS, the Unified Patent Court (UPC) Court of Appeal dismissed the appeal, reinforcing the strict application of procedural rules to ensure legal certainty. The Court conducted an ex officio review of appeal deadlines, confirming that a rejection order for inadmissibility does not exceed the scope of the claims. Furthermore, the judgment reaffirmed established case law stating that the relative financial disparity between claimant and defendant is not a valid criterion under Rule 158 of the Rules of Procedure for determining cost security. This decision underscores the UPC’s commitment to preventing arbitrary judicial treatment and highlights the binding nature of procedural compliance in future litigation involving cost security and admissibility challenges.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC诉讼时，须严守程序期限，避免因技术性违规导致权利丧失。针对费用担保，不能仅凭双方财力差距抗辩，需准备实质性的胜诉可能性证据。建议加强内部IP合规流程，特别是跨境诉讼中的程序管理，以降低因程序失误带来的高额成本风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Litigants before the UPC must prioritize rigorous adherence to procedural timelines and formal requirements, as technical non-compliance can lead to immediate dismissal regardless of substantive merits. Regarding cost security, arguments based solely on financial disparity are unlikely to succeed; instead, parties should focus on demonstrating a strong prima facie case regarding the likelihood of success on the merits. Legal teams should strengthen internal cross-border litigation protocols to manage procedural risks effectively. Monitoring how the Court applies these strict standards in subsequent cases will be crucial for developing robust defense strategies and minimizing exposure to high procedural costs.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院依职权严格审查上诉期限与程序规则，确保法律确定性及非歧视原则", "驳回上诉作为不可受理裁定，不视为超出当事人诉讼请求的范围", "当事人相对财务状况不构成申请费用担保的法定依据，维持既往判例立场"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Court strictly reviews appeal deadlines and procedural rules ex officio to uphold legal certainty and prevent discriminatory or arbitrary judicial administration.", "An order rejecting an appeal as inadmissible is legally distinct and does not constitute a decision exceeding the scope of the parties' specific claims.", "Relative financial differences between parties do not serve as a statutory basis for cost security adjustments under Rule 158, maintaining consistent prior precedent."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["严守UPC程序期限，规避因技术性违规导致的权利丧失风险", "费用担保抗辩需侧重胜诉可能性，而非单纯依赖财力对比"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure strict adherence to UPC procedural deadlines to avoid rights forfeiture due to technical violations.", "Practice note: Substantiate cost security defenses with evidence of merit rather than relying on comparative financial strength.", "What to monitor: Future applications of Rule 158 and ex officio admissibility checks in UPC cost security proceedings."]
core_points_en: The Court strictly reviews appeal deadlines and procedural rules ex officio to uphold legal certainty and prevent discriminatory or arbitrary judicial administration.
An order rejecting an appeal as inadmissible is legally distinct and does not constitute a decision exceeding the scope of the parties' specific claims.
Relative financial differences between parties do not serve as a statutory basis for cost security adjustments under Rule 158, maintaining consistent prior precedent.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure strict adherence to UPC procedural deadlines to avoid rights forfeiture due to technical violations.
Practice note: Substantiate cost security defenses with evidence of merit rather than relying on comparative financial strength.
What to monitor: Future applications of Rule 158 and ex officio admissibility checks in UPC cost security proceedings.

items.title: EPO Enlarged Board Clarifies: Publicly Available Products Are Prior Art, Even If Irreproducible
items.title_zh: EPO扩大上诉委员会：公开产品即便不可再现亦属现有技术
items.title_en: EPO Enlarged Board: Publicly Available Products Are Prior Art Even If Irreproducible
items.summary: The European Patent Office's Enlarged Board has fundamentally altered the landscape of prior art assessment, establishing that products made publicly available before a patent filing date constitute prior art regardless of whether they can be analyzed or reproduced. This landmark decision removes the long-standing reproducibility requirement, making market availability alone sufficient to destroy novelty and significantly raising the stakes for timely patent filings.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2025年7月2日，EPO扩大上诉委员会在G1/23案中裁定，申请日前公开的产品构成现有技术，无论其成分是否可被分析或再现。该决定推翻了G1/92案确立的“可再现性”要求，认为仅市场可获得性即足以破坏新颖性。此举消除了长期存在的法律不确定性，显著提高了及时提交专利申请的重要性，任何上市前公开均可能使后续专利无效。
items.ai_summary_en: On July 2, 2025, the Enlarged Board of Appeal of the European Patent Office issued decision G 1/23, ruling that products made publicly available before a patent application’s filing date constitute prior art under Article 54(2) EPC. This applies regardless of whether their composition or internal structure can be analyzed or reproduced by a skilled person. The decision overturns the long-standing reproducibility requirement established in G 1/92, establishing that market availability alone is sufficient to destroy novelty. This landmark ruling eliminates significant legal uncertainty regarding the status of irreproducible products and substantially raises the stakes for timely patent filings. Consequently, any public disclosure prior to filing, including sales or exhibitions, may invalidate subsequent patent rights, fundamentally altering the landscape of prior art assessment in Europe.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需警惕产品上市前的任何公开行为，包括展会展示或销售，因即使技术细节未完全披露，产品本身也可作为现有技术驳回专利。建议建立严格的保密与申请时间管理机制，确保在申请日前不公开核心技术，避免在新规下丧失新颖性。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision fundamentally shifts the risk profile for product launches in Europe. Companies must now treat any public availability of a product as potentially fatal to future patent claims, irrespective of whether competitors can technically replicate the invention. Strict confidentiality protocols are essential before any market exposure. Organizations should prioritize securing European patent applications before engaging in trade shows, sales, or other public disclosures. Failure to align filing timelines with commercial activities may result in irreversible loss of novelty. Monitoring this new standard is critical for global IP strategy, particularly for industries relying on complex chemical or material compositions where analysis was previously considered a barrier to prior art status.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["推翻G1/92先例，取消现有技术认定中的“可再现性”硬性门槛", "确立市场公开即构成现有技术原则，无论内部结构能否被反向工程", "极大扩展现有技术范围，对专利新颖性审查及申请策略产生深远影响"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Overturns G 1/92 precedent by removing the strict reproducibility threshold for determining prior art status.", "Establishes that market availability constitutes prior art regardless of whether internal structures can be reverse-engineered.", "Significantly expands the scope of prior art, profoundly impacting novelty examination and patent application strategies."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["强化申请前保密管理，严防产品上市导致新颖性丧失风险", "调整全球布局策略，优先在欧洲完成申请后再进行市场推广"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strengthen pre-filing confidentiality management to prevent novelty loss risks from premature product launches.", "Practice note: Adjust global portfolio strategies by prioritizing European patent filings before initiating market promotion or sales.", "What to monitor: How national courts and examiners apply this broader prior art definition to complex, non-reproducible technologies."]
core_points_en: Overturns G 1/92 precedent by removing the strict reproducibility threshold for determining prior art status.
Establishes that market availability constitutes prior art regardless of whether internal structures can be reverse-engineered.
Significantly expands the scope of prior art, profoundly impacting novelty examination and patent application strategies.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strengthen pre-filing confidentiality management to prevent novelty loss risks from premature product launches.
Practice note: Adjust global portfolio strategies by prioritizing European patent filings before initiating market promotion or sales.
What to monitor: How national courts and examiners apply this broader prior art definition to complex, non-reproducible technologies.

items.title: PMAC Advances Operations with New Procedural Rules and Launch of Case Management System
items.title_zh: UPC专利调解仲裁中心发布新规并上线案件管理系统
items.title_en: UPC Patent Mediation Centre Adopts Rules and Launches Case Management System
items.summary: The Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre (PMAC) of the Unified Patent Court (UPC) has taken significant steps toward full operational readiness,
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）行政委员会于2026年4月正式通过专利调解和仲裁中心（PMAC）的程序规则，并于5月12日上线专用案件管理系统（CCMS）。PMAC旨在提供调解、仲裁等替代性争议解决服务，涵盖欧洲专利及标准必要专利（SEP）纠纷。此举标志着PMAC具备全面运营能力，为权利人提供了高效灵活的专利争议解决新途径，进一步完善了UPC生态系统。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Administrative Committee formally adopted the procedural rules for the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre (PMAC) in April 2026, establishing its legal foundation. Subsequently, on May 12, 2026, PMAC launched its dedicated Case Management System, enabling electronic submission of alternative dispute resolution requests. This development marks a significant step toward full operational readiness for the Centre. PMAC offers mediation, arbitration, and expert determination services covering European patents, Unitary Patents, and Standard Essential Patent disputes. These structured mechanisms provide efficient, flexible options within the UPC ecosystem, aiming to enhance access to effective patent dispute resolution alongside traditional litigation pathways.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧涉及专利纠纷时，可关注UPC-PMAC提供的调解与仲裁渠道。相比传统诉讼，ADR程序更具灵活性与保密性，有助于降低维权成本并加速SEP许可谈判进程。建议企业评估将部分非核心或复杂跨国争议转入PMAC处理，以优化全球知识产权战略布局。
items.ai_insight_en: Parties involved in European patent disputes should evaluate the UPC-PMAC as a viable alternative to traditional litigation. The ADR mechanisms offer greater flexibility and confidentiality, which can significantly reduce enforcement costs and accelerate resolution timelines. For Standard Essential Patent licensing negotiations, these channels may facilitate more constructive dialogue and potentially yield favorable settlement terms. Stakeholders are advised to assess whether non-core or complex cross-border conflicts could benefit from PMAC procedures, thereby optimizing global intellectual property strategy and resource allocation.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC行政委员会正式批准PMAC程序规则，奠定法律与运营基础", "PMAC案件管理系统正式上线，支持电子提交替代性争议解决请求", "服务范围涵盖欧洲专利及SEP/FRAND案件，提供多元ADR选项"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UPC Administrative Committee approved PMAC procedural rules, establishing the legal and operational framework for its activities.", "PMAC Case Management System went live on May 12, supporting electronic submissions for alternative dispute resolution proceedings.", "Service scope covers European patents and SEP/FRAND cases, offering diverse ADR options including mediation and arbitration."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用PMAC的ADR机制可降低在欧专利诉讼成本并提升解决效率", "针对SEP/FRAND纠纷，可探索通过调解仲裁达成更优许可条件"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Utilizing PMAC ADR mechanisms can lower European patent litigation costs and improve resolution efficiency.", "Practice note: For SEP/FRAND disputes, explore mediation and arbitration to achieve optimized licensing conditions.", "What to monitor: Track the commencement of mediation services in late May 2026 and subsequent arbitration rollout schedules."]
core_points_en: UPC Administrative Committee approved PMAC procedural rules, establishing the legal and operational framework for its activities.
PMAC Case Management System went live on May 12, supporting electronic submissions for alternative dispute resolution proceedings.
Service scope covers European patents and SEP/FRAND cases, offering diverse ADR options including mediation and arbitration.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Utilizing PMAC ADR mechanisms can lower European patent litigation costs and improve resolution efficiency.
Practice note: For SEP/FRAND disputes, explore mediation and arbitration to achieve optimized licensing conditions.
What to monitor: Track the commencement of mediation services in late May 2026 and subsequent arbitration rollout schedules.

items.title: Creating ongoing awareness among entrepreneurs about the importance of IP
items.title_zh: BOIP持续向企业家普及知识产权重要性
items.title_en: BOIP Launches New Campaign to Boost Entrepreneur IP Awareness in Benelux
items.summary: Unfortunately, many entrepreneurs are still unfamiliar with intellectual property (IP).
items.ai_summary_zh: 比荷卢知识产权局（BOIP）指出许多企业家对知识产权（IP）缺乏了解或认为其复杂昂贵。通过“Create, Protect, Benefit”平台及合作伙伴，BOIP旨在展示IP在加强商业地位、促进合作及加速融资方面的优势。该计划已运行六年，2025年营销活动覆盖超百万人次。新campaign旨在持续提升广大企业家对IP的认知，推动IP成为比荷卢地区商业计划的组成部分。
items.ai_summary_en: The Benelux Office for Intellectual Property (BOIP) highlights that many entrepreneurs remain unfamiliar with intellectual property or perceive it as complex and costly. Through the 'Create, Protect, Benefit' platform, BOIP and its partners aim to demonstrate IP's strategic value in strengthening business positions, facilitating collaborations, and accelerating access to financing. This initiative has operated for six years across the Netherlands and Belgium, involving partners such as FPS Economy, the Netherlands Patent Office, VLAIO, and Wallonie Entreprendre. In 2025, marketing efforts generated over one million impressions, significantly expanding reach. A new campaign launches this month to continuously raise awareness among a broad entrepreneur base. The ultimate goal, aligned with the Strategic Plan BOIP 2030, is to make IP an integral component of every business plan within the Benelux region.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国企业应重视比荷卢地区的IP基础认知建设，利用官方免费资源降低合规成本。建议主动参与此类意识提升活动，将IP纳入早期商业规划，以优化融资环境并增强本地化合作的信任度，避免因地方法规认知不足导致的市场准入障碍。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in the Benelux should leverage official BOIP resources to mitigate compliance costs and enhance local market integration. Integrating IP considerations into early-stage business planning can optimize financing opportunities and strengthen trust in localized collaborations. Companies are advised to actively engage with these awareness initiatives to prevent market entry barriers caused by insufficient understanding of regional regulations. Utilizing free official guides and success stories can provide accessible pathways to effective IP protection, supporting sustainable growth and innovation strategies within the European framework.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["BOIP联合多方伙伴推广IP价值，消除企业家对IP复杂昂贵的误解", "强调IP在强化商业地位、促进合作及获取融资中的关键作用", "新营销战役旨在扩大覆盖面，持续提升比荷卢地区企业家IP意识"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["BOIP collaborates with multiple partners to promote IP value and dispel misconceptions regarding complexity and high costs.", "The initiative emphasizes IP's critical role in reinforcing commercial standing, enabling partnerships, and securing funding.", "A new marketing campaign aims to expand coverage and sustainably elevate IP awareness among Benelux entrepreneurs."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用BOIP官方资源降低IP合规成本，优化比荷卢市场融资环境", "将IP纳入早期商业规划，增强本地化合作信任度与市场准入能力"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage BOIP official resources to reduce IP compliance costs and optimize financing environments in the Benelux market.", "Practice note: Integrate IP strategy into early business planning to enhance trust in local collaborations and improve market access capabilities.", "What to monitor: Ongoing BOIP marketing campaigns and updated practical guides on IP protection for entrepreneurs in the region."]
core_points_en: BOIP collaborates with multiple partners to promote IP value and dispel misconceptions regarding complexity and high costs.
The initiative emphasizes IP's critical role in reinforcing commercial standing, enabling partnerships, and securing funding.
A new marketing campaign aims to expand coverage and sustainably elevate IP awareness among Benelux entrepreneurs.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage BOIP official resources to reduce IP compliance costs and optimize financing environments in the Benelux market.
Practice note: Integrate IP strategy into early business planning to enhance trust in local collaborations and improve market access capabilities.
What to monitor: Ongoing BOIP marketing campaigns and updated practical guides on IP protection for entrepreneurs in the region.

items.title: Central Division Paris accepts revocation action against expired patent
items.title_zh: UPC巴黎分部：过期专利撤销之诉若具法律利益仍属可受理
items.title_en: UPC Paris: Revocation Action Admissible for Expired Patent if Legal Interest Persists
items.summary: Huntsman (Europe) BV and Huntsman Holland BV v BASF SE — Central Division Paris, 22 April 2026 (UPC_CFI_461/2025))
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC巴黎中央分部在Huntsman诉BASF案中裁定，即使涉案专利已过期，只要原告因追索过往侵权损害赔偿而保留合法法律利益，撤销诉讼即具可受理性。法院重申在创造性评估中不受专利自身技术问题表述限制，可独立界定客观技术问题。此外，辅助请求的审查仅限于针对修改后权利要求的具体无效攻击，不自动沿用对授权权利要求的异议。
items.ai_summary_en: In Huntsman v BASF, the UPC Central Division Paris ruled that a revocation action remains admissible even after patent expiry, provided the claimant retains a legitimate legal interest, such as pursuing damages for past infringement. The Court confirmed it is not bound by the objective technical problem stated in the patent specification and may independently define this problem during inventive-step analysis. Furthermore, regarding auxiliary requests, the Court established that only specific invalidity attacks directed at amended claims are considered. Objections raised against granted claims do not automatically carry over to modified versions, requiring parties to precisely target their arguments in amendment proceedings.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需警惕UPC程序灵活性，即便专利过期，对手仍可借撤销之诉清除障碍以索赔。应对时须精准论证法律利益缺失，并在答复辅助请求时聚焦具体修改点，避免异议范围不当扩大，强化程序抗辩策略。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision highlights the procedural flexibility of the UPC, where expired patents can still be challenged to clear obstacles for subsequent damages claims. Parties must rigorously assess whether a plaintiff retains a legitimate legal interest to block such actions early. In response strategies, defendants should carefully tailor their defenses for auxiliary requests, focusing strictly on the specific amendments made. This prevents the unintended expansion of invalidity grounds and ensures that objections to original claims do not automatically prejudice modified versions, thereby strengthening procedural defense positions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["过期专利撤销案若为追索过往侵权赔偿，因具备法律利益仍可被受理", "法院在创造性分析中有权独立界定客观技术问题，不受专利文本束缚", "辅助请求审查限于针对修改权利要求的具体无效攻击，不自动继承异议"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Revocation actions remain admissible post-expiry if the claimant pursues damages for alleged past infringement.", "Courts may independently determine the objective technical problem and are not confined to the wording used in the patent specification.", "Review of auxiliary requests is limited to specific invalidity attacks on amended claims, without automatic inheritance of prior objections."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕过期专利撤销风险，积极抗辩原告缺乏合法法律利益以阻断诉讼", "优化辅助请求答复策略，严格限定审查范围，防止无效理由不当扩展"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess whether plaintiffs retain legal interest via pending damages claims to challenge admissibility of post-expiry revocation actions.", "Practice note: Tailor responses to auxiliary requests by addressing only specific amendments, preventing automatic extension of objections from granted claims.", "What to monitor: How UPC divisions apply independent definitions of the objective technical problem in future inventive-step assessments."]
core_points_en: Revocation actions remain admissible post-expiry if the claimant pursues damages for alleged past infringement.
Courts may independently determine the objective technical problem and are not confined to the wording used in the patent specification.
Review of auxiliary requests is limited to specific invalidity attacks on amended claims, without automatic inheritance of prior objections.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess whether plaintiffs retain legal interest via pending damages claims to challenge admissibility of post-expiry revocation actions.
Practice note: Tailor responses to auxiliary requests by addressing only specific amendments, preventing automatic extension of objections from granted claims.
What to monitor: How UPC divisions apply independent definitions of the objective technical problem in future inventive-step assessments.

items.title: Mind the SEP: UKIPO signals upcoming SEP reform
items.title_zh: 英专局信号：即将推进标准必要专利改革
items.title_en: UKIPO Signals Upcoming Standard Essential Patent Regulatory Reforms
items.summary: On 11 May 2026, the UK Intellectual Property Office (UKIPO) published its Corporate Plan for 2026–2027, outlining its policy priorities for the coming…
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年5月11日，英国知识产权局（UKIPO）发布2026-2027企业计划，明确将标准必要专利（SEP）作为政策重点。针对当前生态系统存在的许可不透明、必要性不确定及争议解决成本高企等结构性挑战，UKIPO计划在2026年完成政策制定并推动监管框架变革。此举旨在提升英国创新吸引力，虽无即时立法，但预示未来将在许可透明度、SEP必要性认定及纠纷效率方面进行改革。
items.ai_summary_en: On 11 May 2026, the UK Intellectual Property Office published its Corporate Plan for 2026–2027, identifying standard essential patents as a key policy priority. The plan acknowledges longstanding structural challenges within the current ecosystem, specifically limited licensing transparency, uncertainty regarding patent essentiality, and costly dispute resolution mechanisms that disproportionately impact small and medium enterprises. While no immediate legislative changes are introduced, the UKIPO intends to complete policy development in 2026 to drive regulatory framework reforms. These initiatives aim to enhance the UK’s attractiveness as a leading jurisdiction for innovation by addressing inefficiencies and improving certainty for stakeholders involved in SEP licensing and enforcement.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国通信及科技企业在英面临更严格的SEP合规环境。建议密切关注UKIPO后续政策细则，提前评估现有许可协议的透明度与必要性条款风险。应加强内部SEP管理能力，积极参与行业咨询，以应对潜在的监管变化及更高的争议解决效率要求，避免在新一轮改革中处于被动地位。
items.ai_insight_en: Stakeholders engaged in SEP licensing and enforcement should anticipate a stricter compliance environment driven by upcoming UK regulatory adjustments. It is prudent to evaluate existing licensing agreements for transparency and essentiality clause risks ahead of formal rule changes. Organizations should strengthen internal SEP management capabilities and actively participate in industry consultations to shape emerging standards. Proactive engagement will help mitigate potential liabilities and ensure readiness for more efficient, yet rigorous, dispute resolution processes expected under the new framework.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UKIPO将SEP列为核心政策领域，致力于解决许可不透明及必要性不确定性问题", "计划于2026年完成政策制定，推动监管框架改革以优化争议解决机制", "改革旨在降低中小企业成本，维持英国作为全球技术创新领先司法管辖区的吸引力"]

items.title: UPC: Late filed physical evidence cannot clarify the content of an earlier filed brochure
items.title_zh: UPC裁定：迟交实物证据不得用于澄清先前提交的宣传册内容
items.title_en: UPC Vienna Division Excludes Late Physical Evidence Clarifying Prior Art Brochure Disclosure
items.summary: In a recent decision from the Vienna Local Division of the Unified Patent Court (UPC), the Court maintained EP 3705415, taking a strict...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）维也纳地方法院在Messerle诉Sabert案中维持涉案专利有效。被告Sabert试图通过迟交实物样品来澄清商业宣传册中产品特征以攻击专利新颖性。法院裁定宣传册为独立现有技术，实物证据不能用于补充或重构其披露内容，以防事后诸葛亮式解读。因该证据实质引入新的无效理由且提交过晚，法院拒绝采纳，强调证据必须基于现有论点发展而非引入新攻击。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Vienna Local Division maintained European Patent EP 3705415 in infringement proceedings between Messerle and Sabert. The defendant, Sabert, challenged the patent's novelty based on a commercial brochure but submitted physical product samples late to clarify features not clearly discernible in the brochure's two-dimensional images. The Court rejected this evidence, ruling that the brochure constitutes standalone prior art evaluated by the skilled person without external supplementation. Allowing physical evidence would risk reconstructing the disclosure with hindsight. Furthermore, the Court determined that the late submission effectively introduced a new line of attack based on alleged public prior use rather than developing existing arguments regarding the document's content. Consequently, the evidence was inadmissible, and the patent was upheld as the brochure alone did not unambiguously disclose all claimed features.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在UPC诉讼中需严格遵循举证时限与策略一致性。针对新颖性挑战，应确保书面证据本身清晰完整，避免依赖后续实物补强。若主张公开使用，须早期明确提出并单独举证，防止因混淆证据性质导致关键证据被排除，从而丧失无效机会。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision underscores the UPC's rigorous approach to procedural discipline and the admissibility of late-filed evidence. Parties must ensure that documentary prior art stands on its own merits without relying on subsequent physical exhibits to interpret ambiguous features. Attempting to bridge gaps in written disclosures using physical evidence risks being characterized as an impermissible attempt to introduce a new attack, such as public use, at a late stage. Litigants should carefully distinguish between clarifying a document's inherent disclosure and asserting separate grounds for invalidity. Early identification of the specific legal basis for each piece of evidence is critical to avoid procedural exclusion and preserve the validity of the challenge.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院拒绝采纳迟交的实物证据，因其旨在澄清而非补充先前提交的宣传册披露内容", "宣传册作为独立现有技术，其披露范围由本领域技术人员直接明确认定，禁止事后重构", "迟交证据若实质引入全新无效攻击路径（如公开使用），将因程序不当被排除"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Late-filed physical samples were excluded because they aimed to clarify, rather than supplement, the disclosure of an earlier filed commercial brochure.", "A commercial brochure is treated as independent prior art; its scope is defined by what the skilled person directly derives, prohibiting hindsight reconstruction via external obje…", "Evidence submitted late that introduces a fundamentally new invalidity ground, such as public prior use, is procedurally inadmissible if it does not develop existing arguments."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC对迟交证据持严格态度，企业需确保书面证据自身具备充分清晰度", "区分不同无效理由的证据提交策略，避免混合导致程序性驳回"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure documentary prior art is self-sufficient; do not rely on late physical exhibits to interpret ambiguous visual disclosures in novelty attacks.", "Practice note: Clearly distinguish between arguments based on document disclosure and those based on public use to prevent late submissions from being deemed inadmissible new atta…", "What to monitor: UPC divisions' consistent application of strict admissibility rules regarding late evidence that attempts to reconstruct prior art disclosures with hindsight."]
core_points_en: Late-filed physical samples were excluded because they aimed to clarify, rather than supplement, the disclosure of an earlier filed commercial brochure.
A commercial brochure is treated as independent prior art; its scope is defined by what the skilled person directly derives, prohibiting hindsight reconstruction via external obje…
Evidence submitted late that introduces a fundamentally new invalidity ground, such as public prior use, is procedurally inadmissible if it does not develop existing arguments.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure documentary prior art is self-sufficient; do not rely on late physical exhibits to interpret ambiguous visual disclosures in novelty attacks.
Practice note: Clearly distinguish between arguments based on document disclosure and those based on public use to prevent late submissions from being deemed inadmissible new atta…
What to monitor: UPC divisions' consistent application of strict admissibility rules regarding late evidence that attempts to reconstruct prior art disclosures with hindsight.

items.title: HP prevails in Munich in toner cartridge dispute against Ninestar
items.title_zh: HP在慕尼黑墨盒专利纠纷中胜诉Ninestar
items.title_en: HP Secures Victory in Munich Toner Cartridge Patent Dispute Against Ninestar Group
items.summary: In a series of lawsuits concerning various patents, Hewlett Packard Development is taking action at Munich Regional Court against several European and Chinese companies of the Ninestar Group. They all sell printer cartridges on platforms such as Amazon. According to HP, these cartridges infringe HP’s patents. After the court imposed a sales ban on CaiLuo last […] The post HP prevails in Munich in toner cartridge dispute against Ninestar appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 惠普开发公司在慕尼黑地区法院对纳思达集团多家欧中企业发起系列专利诉讼，指控其通过亚马逊等平台销售的打印机墨盒侵犯惠普专利。法院已对其中一家名为CaiLuo的公司实施销售禁令。此案反映了跨国企业在欧洲针对替代耗材市场的严格知识产权保护行动，凸显了当地司法机构在认定专利侵权及执行禁令方面的效率与力度。
items.ai_summary_en: Hewlett Packard Development has initiated a series of patent infringement lawsuits at the Munich Regional Court against multiple European and Chinese entities within the Ninestar Group. The litigation targets printer cartridges sold via online platforms such as Amazon, which HP alleges infringe its proprietary patents. In a significant development, the court has already imposed a sales ban on one of the defendants, CaiLuo. This case underscores the rigorous enforcement of intellectual property rights in Europe regarding the aftermarket consumables sector. It highlights the efficiency and decisive nature of local judicial authorities in determining patent infringement and executing preliminary injunctions, signaling a strict stance against unauthorized compatible products.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，德国后续是否会围绕这类执法动作给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This enforcement action illustrates the heightened risks for manufacturers and distributors of compatible consumables operating within the German market. Rights holders are increasingly leveraging the efficiency of German courts to secure rapid injunctive relief against alleged infringers. For industry participants, conducting thorough Freedom-to-Operate analyses prior to market entry is critical to mitigate exposure to such litigation. Furthermore, when facing infringement claims, relying solely on platform-level dispute mechanisms is insufficient; parties should actively engage in judicial proceedings and consider parallel invalidity actions to effectively defend their commercial interests.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["惠普在慕尼黑法院起诉纳思达集团侵犯多项专利", "涉案产品为通过亚马逊销售的打印机兼容墨盒", "法院已对部分被告实施临时销售禁令"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["HP filed multiple patent infringement suits in Munich against various Ninestar Group companies across Europe and China.", "The disputed products are compatible printer cartridges distributed through major e-commerce platforms like Amazon.", "The Munich Regional Court has issued temporary sales injunctions against specific defendants, including CaiLuo."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["出口企业需强化德国市场专利FTO尽职调查", "应对策略应结合无效宣告与积极应诉机制"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Manufacturers of compatible consumables must prioritize robust FTO due diligence before entering the German market to avoid costly injunctions.", "Practice note: Defense strategies should integrate active litigation participation with parallel invalidity proceedings rather than depending exclusively on e-commerce platform ap…", "What to monitor: Ongoing developments in this multi-party litigation and subsequent rulings by the Munich Regional Court regarding patent validity and infringement scope."]
core_points_en: HP filed multiple patent infringement suits in Munich against various Ninestar Group companies across Europe and China.
The disputed products are compatible printer cartridges distributed through major e-commerce platforms like Amazon.
The Munich Regional Court has issued temporary sales injunctions against specific defendants, including CaiLuo.

items.title: LD Helsinki, April 29, 2026, Decision On Infringement And Validity, UPC_CFI_214/2023, UPC_CFI_403/2025
items.title_zh: UPC赫尔辛基分院：专利侵权与有效性裁决及等同原则适用
items.title_en: UPC Helsinki Adopts Hague Equivalence Approach and Limits Revocation Review Scope
items.summary: The Helsinki Local Division of the Unified Patent Court examined infringement proceedings involving EP 3 295 663, with AIM Sport Development AG asserting patent rights against multiple TGI Sport entities across European jurisdictions. The case included a counterclaim for revocation, requiring the court to analyze validity under Articles 54, 56, and 123(2) EPC alongside infringement claims under Article 69 EPC.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）赫尔辛基地方分院就EP 3 295 663号专利作出侵权与有效性判决。在缺乏上诉法院先例时，法院采纳海牙分院的等同侵权判定路径，强调等同标准需累积满足，任一不成立即无侵权。针对撤销反诉，法院指出若当事人未充分论证“新增事项”，仅需回应已 substantiated 的部分。该案明确了UPC内部司法一致性的重要性及举证责任边界。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Helsinki Local Division issued a decision on infringement and validity concerning EP 3 295 663. In proceedings brought by AIM Sport Development AG against multiple TGI Sport entities, the court addressed key procedural and substantive issues. Absent Court of Appeal precedent on equivalence, the Division adopted the approach established by The Hague Local Division to ensure legal certainty. The judgment clarified that equivalence criteria are cumulative; failure to meet any single criterion precludes a finding of infringement. Regarding the counterclaim for revocation, the court ruled it would only address allegations supported by substantiated arguments. Since the defendants provided minimal reasoning for their added matter claim, the court’s analysis was correspondingly limited, highlighting strict boundaries for party submissions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC诉讼时，需密切关注各地方分院对等同原则的司法倾向，尤其是海牙、赫尔辛基等关键分院。在提出无效抗辩时，必须提供详尽的技术比对与法律论证，避免仅凭形式主张导致被驳回。建议建立跨法域的专利风险预警机制，统一应诉策略以应对UPC日益增强的判例约束力。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision underscores the importance of judicial consistency among UPC Local Divisions, particularly regarding the doctrine of equivalents. Practitioners should note that while divisions may look to peers like The Hague for guidance, the cumulative nature of equivalence tests offers potential defense avenues. Furthermore, the ruling on Article 123(2) EPC emphasizes that formalistic invalidity claims lacking detailed technical or legal substantiation will receive limited judicial attention. Parties must ensure robust, evidence-based arguments in counterclaims to secure comprehensive court consideration, avoiding reliance on bare assertions that may be dismissed without full analysis.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["无上诉先例时，地方法院参照海牙分院等同判定路径以保法律确定性", "等同侵权判定标准具有累积性，任一要件缺失即认定不构成等同侵权", "法院仅对当事人提供实质论证的撤销理由进行回应，简化审理焦点"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Local divisions may adopt peer division approaches, such as The Hague’s, to ensure legal certainty when no Court of Appeal precedent exists.", "Equivalence infringement criteria are cumulative; if any single criterion is unmet, the court need not assess remaining factors.", "Courts respond to revocation allegations only to the extent parties present substantiated arguments, limiting judicial review scope."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注UPC地方分院等同原则司法倾向，统一跨法域应诉策略", "无效抗辩需提供实质技术论证，避免形式主张被法院忽略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Understand how Local Divisions harmonize equivalence assessments to predict litigation outcomes across jurisdictions.", "Practice note: Ensure invalidity counterclaims include detailed technical comparisons and legal arguments to avoid summary dismissal.", "What to monitor: Track evolving case law from key divisions like The Hague and Helsinki regarding cumulative equivalence criteria."]
core_points_en: Local divisions may adopt peer division approaches, such as The Hague’s, to ensure legal certainty when no Court of Appeal precedent exists.
Equivalence infringement criteria are cumulative; if any single criterion is unmet, the court need not assess remaining factors.
Courts respond to revocation allegations only to the extent parties present substantiated arguments, limiting judicial review scope.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Understand how Local Divisions harmonize equivalence assessments to predict litigation outcomes across jurisdictions.
Practice note: Ensure invalidity counterclaims include detailed technical comparisons and legal arguments to avoid summary dismissal.
What to monitor: Track evolving case law from key divisions like The Hague and Helsinki regarding cumulative equivalence criteria.

items.title: LD Düsseldorf, April 28, 2026, Procedural Order, UPC_CFI_869/2025
items.title_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫地院2026年4月程序令：关于提交文件可采性的裁决
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf Order: Admissibility of Late Rejoinder Arguments on Amendment Motion
items.summary: The admissibility of the documents and arguments submitted is a question of fact. Whether the defendant’s submissions in its rejoinder regarding the motion to amend are relevant...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）杜塞尔多夫地方分院发布程序令（案号UPC_CFI_869/2025），涉及大日本印刷诉Zapp等公司专利侵权案。法院裁定，被告在反驳中针对修改动议提交的论据是否相关及是否逾期，属事实问题，需待口头程序结束后方能确定。依据武器平等原则，法院允许原告就被告的事实陈述补充评论，作为预防性措施，且不 prejudge 最终的可采性决定。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) Düsseldorf Local Division issued a procedural order in case UPC_CFI_869/2025, involving Dai Nippon Printing Co., Ltd. against Zapp AG and Zapp Precision Metals GmbH. The court addressed the admissibility of documents and arguments submitted by the defendant in its rejoinder regarding a motion to amend. It determined that assessing whether these submissions are relevant or late is a factual question requiring thorough analysis, which can only be conclusively resolved at the end of oral proceedings. To uphold the principle of equality of arms, the court granted the plaintiff an additional opportunity to comment on the defendant’s statement of facts. This measure is precautionary and without prejudice to the final decision on whether the defendant’s additional arguments will be admitted into the record.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC诉讼时，应高度重视程序规则的灵活性。即便对方提交材料可能逾期，法院仍可能基于公平原则给予我方回应机会。建议企业建立快速响应机制，充分利用程序赋予的评论权，同时密切关注法官对“武器平等”原则的适用尺度，以优化诉讼策略。
items.ai_insight_en: This procedural order highlights the UPC’s flexible approach to managing late submissions and amendment motions. Parties should recognize that courts may prioritize procedural fairness over strict deadlines when balancing the interests of both sides. Litigants must remain agile, prepared to utilize opportunities for supplementary comments effectively. Establishing rapid response mechanisms allows teams to address unexpected factual assertions promptly. Furthermore, monitoring how judges apply the 'equality of arms' principle provides strategic insights into evidentiary standards. Understanding this judicial discretion helps optimize litigation strategies, ensuring that procedural rights are fully leveraged to counter potential surprises from opposing counsel during complex patent disputes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["被告反驳中的修改动议论据可采性属事实问题，需结合案情全面分析", "基于武器平等原则，原告获准对被告新增事实陈述进行补充评论", "最终可采性判定将推迟至口头程序结束时作出，确保程序公正"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Admissibility of defendant's rejoinder arguments on amendment motions is a factual issue requiring full case analysis.", "Plaintiff granted supplementary comment rights on new facts to ensure equality of arms during proceedings.", "Final ruling on late evidence admissibility deferred until conclusion of oral hearings to ensure fairness."]

items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage procedural rules to secure supplementary comment opportunities, balancing opposing parties' surprise evidence tactics.", "Practice note: Monitor judicial discretion on late evidence admissibility to refine internal response workflows and litigation readiness.", "What to monitor: Track evolving standards for 'equality of arms' applications to anticipate judicial attitudes toward procedural delays."]

Plaintiff granted supplementary comment rights on new facts to ensure equality of arms during proceedings.

items.title: Court Of Appeal, Standing Judge, April 28, 2026, Order On Request For Discretionary Review, UPC_CoA_56/2026
items.title_zh: UPC上诉庭裁定：错过期限的人为错误可获追溯延期
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal: Human Error Under Systemic Checks Warrants Retroactive Term Extension
items.summary: The Court of Appeal examines a request for discretionary review in a patent dispute between Optopol Technology and Topcon Corporation concerning EP 2 845 534. The case involves interpretation of procedural rules governing standing and review processes under the Unified Patent Court framework.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本案系Optopol与Topcon在统一专利法院（UPC）的专利纠纷。上诉庭审查了针对EP 2845534号专利的程序性请求。法院认定，尽管代理人助理系统录入失误导致错过期限，但律所已建立核查机制，该人为错误属可原谅情形。上诉庭支持地方法院决定，允许在权利恢复请求期内隐含提交追溯期限延长申请，强调此类申请应尽早提出，但在特定未决判例背景下具有合理性。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal examined a request for discretionary review in patent litigation between Optopol Technology and Topcon Corporation concerning EP 2 845 534. The dispute centered on procedural rules governing standing and the re-establishment of rights. The Local Division had found that a missed deadline resulted from an excusable human error, specifically an assistant’s incorrect data entry, despite the law firm having established systemic checks. The Court of Appeal upheld this determination as reasonable. It further validated the implicit filing of a retroactive term extension request under Rule 9.3 RoP within the timeframe for re-establishment of rights. While emphasizing that such requests should generally be made promptly, the Court acknowledged the reasonableness of the timing given the pending precedent in Angelalign v. Angel Technology at that stage.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC诉讼时，需建立严格的期限双重核查机制以防范人为失误。若遇不可抗力或合理疏忽，应及时利用“可原谅错误”条款申请救济。同时，注意程序请求的时效性与独立性，避免过度依赖替代性请求策略，确保合规操作以降低专利失效风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision clarifies the threshold for excusable error under the Unified Patent Court framework, balancing strict procedural compliance with practical realities. Parties should ensure their docketing systems include redundant verification layers to demonstrate due care when relying on human error defenses. While alternative pleading strategies, such as implicitly combining term extension requests with re-establishment applications, may be accepted, practitioners must prioritize promptness. Monitoring evolving case law regarding procedural discretion remains essential to navigate complex timelines effectively and mitigate risks of patent lapse due to administrative oversights.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC上诉庭认定代理人系统性核查下的人为失误属可原谅错误", "支持在权利恢复期限内隐含提交追溯期限延长申请的合理性", "明确依据程序规则第9.3条，追溯延期请求原则上应尽早提出"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Human error causing missed deadlines is excusable if robust systemic verification measures are already implemented by representatives.", "Implicit requests for retroactive term extension filed within the re-establishment period are procedurally valid under specific circumstances.", "Rule 9.3 RoP requires retroactive term extension requests to be submitted as soon as possible, though context matters."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立严格期限双重核查机制，防范人为失误导致专利失效", "善用“可原谅错误”条款及时申请救济，确保程序合规"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Implement redundant docketing checks to substantiate 'excusable error' claims during procedural disputes.", "Practice note: File retroactive term extension requests promptly; avoid over-relying on implicit alternative pleadings.", "What to monitor: Developments in UPC discretionary review standards regarding procedural defaults and human error exceptions."]
core_points_en: Human error causing missed deadlines is excusable if robust systemic verification measures are already implemented by representatives.
Implicit requests for retroactive term extension filed within the re-establishment period are procedurally valid under specific circumstances.
Rule 9.3 RoP requires retroactive term extension requests to be submitted as soon as possible, though context matters.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Implement redundant docketing checks to substantiate 'excusable error' claims during procedural disputes.
Practice note: File retroactive term extension requests promptly; avoid over-relying on implicit alternative pleadings.
What to monitor: Developments in UPC discretionary review standards regarding procedural defaults and human error exceptions.

items.title: LD Helsinki, April 29, 2026, Order Disposing Of Provisional Measures Application, ACT_551054/2023
items.title_zh: UPC赫尔辛基地院：临时措施申请失去目的时予以处置
items.title_en: UPC Helsinki: Disposing Provisional Measures When Devoid of Purpose Under Rule 360
items.summary: If a provisional measures application has become devoid of purpose and hence there is no longer need to adjudicate on it, the Court shall dispose of it based on R. 360 RoP...
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年4月29日，UPC赫尔辛基地方法院就ACT_551054/2023案发布命令。法院依据《程序规则》第360条裁定，若临时措施申请已失去目的且无需再行裁决，应予以处置。若当事人请求，法院可作出费用决定，并酌情考虑实体案件已知结果。本案涉及AIM Sport Development AG与多家TGI Sport实体关于EP 3 295 663号专利的争议。
items.ai_summary_en: On April 29, 2026, the UPC Helsinki Local Division issued an order in case ACT_551054/2023 regarding European Patent EP 3 295 663. The Court applied Rule 360 of the Rules of Procedure to dispose of an application for provisional measures that had become devoid of purpose, determining that further adjudication was unnecessary. The dispute involved applicant AIM Sport Development AG and multiple respondents within the TGI Sport group. Crucially, the ruling clarifies that upon request by the parties, the Court may issue a cost decision. In making this determination, the Court retains the discretion to consider the known outcome of the main proceedings on the merits. This procedural guidance highlights the mechanism for terminating interim relief proceedings when the underlying necessity ceases, while allowing for equitable allocation of legal costs based on the substantive case resolution.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC临时禁令时，需密切关注程序进展。若主案胜诉或和解，应及时主张临时措施失去目的以终止程序，避免不必要的费用支出。建议结合实体判决结果，积极向法院申请费用分担，优化跨境知识产权诉讼的成本控制策略。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision provides critical procedural clarity for litigants before the Unified Patent Court. When a main action is resolved through settlement or final judgment, interim relief applications often lose their practical utility. Parties should proactively seek dismissal of such measures under Rule 360 to avoid unnecessary procedural steps. Furthermore, requesting a cost decision allows courts to factor in the substantive case outcome, potentially leading to more favorable cost allocations. Strategic management of these procedural transitions is essential for optimizing litigation budgets and minimizing exposure to adverse cost orders in cross-border patent disputes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["临时措施申请若失去目的，UPC法院依R.360条应予处置", "法院可依当事人请求裁定诉讼费用，参考实体案已知结果", "本案涉及EP 3 295 663号专利，原告为AIM Sport，被告为多家公司"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UPC Courts must dispose of provisional measures applications under Rule 360 RoP if they become devoid of purpose.", "Courts may rule on costs upon party request, considering the known outcome of the main proceedings on the merits.", "The case involves patent EP 3 295 663 with AIM Sport Development AG as applicant against various TGI Sport entities."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注临时措施目的丧失情形，及时申请终止程序", "利用实体案结果争取有利费用裁定，降低诉讼成本"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Proactively seek dismissal of interim measures when the main case resolves to avoid wasted resources.", "Practice note: Request cost decisions referencing the merits outcome to optimize financial recovery or limit liability.", "What to monitor: Track the status of main proceedings to identify when provisional measures become devoid of purpose."]
core_points_en: UPC Courts must dispose of provisional measures applications under Rule 360 RoP if they become devoid of purpose.
Courts may rule on costs upon party request, considering the known outcome of the main proceedings on the merits.
The case involves patent EP 3 295 663 with AIM Sport Development AG as applicant against various TGI Sport entities.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Proactively seek dismissal of interim measures when the main case resolves to avoid wasted resources.
Practice note: Request cost decisions referencing the merits outcome to optimize financial recovery or limit liability.
What to monitor: Track the status of main proceedings to identify when provisional measures become devoid of purpose.

items.title: Partial success for Millenium Pharma in battle over Velcade in Paris
items.title_zh: 巴黎法院裁定：Millennium Pharma在Velcade专利战中部分胜诉
items.title_en: Paris Court Rules Partially for Millennium Pharma in Post-Expiry Velcade Patent Dispute
items.summary: Millennium Pharmaceuticals and Cilag accuse several generic drug companies of infringing patents EP 2 251 344 B1 and EP 3 078 667 B1. Both protect a formulation of boronic acid compounds. They form the basis of Millennium’s drug Velcade with the active ingredient bortezomib. The drug treats multiple myeloma and mantle cell lymphoma. Since the patents expired […] The post Partial success for Millenium Pharma in battle over Velcade in Paris appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: Millennium Pharmaceuticals与Cilag指控多家仿制药企侵犯其保护硼酸化合物制剂的欧洲专利EP 2 251 344 B1和EP 3 078 667 B1，该专利是抗癌药Velcade的基础。巴黎司法法院作出裁决，原告获得部分胜诉。鉴于相关专利已过期，此案主要涉及过往侵权行为的法律责任认定及赔偿问题，体现了原研药企在专利到期后仍通过诉讼维护市场利益的策略。
items.ai_summary_en: Millennium Pharmaceuticals and Cilag initiated infringement proceedings against several generic manufacturers regarding European patents EP 2 251 344 B1 and EP 3 078 667 B1. These patents protect specific boronic acid compound formulations used in the anticancer drug Velcade, which treats multiple myeloma and mantle cell lymphoma. The Paris Judicial Court delivered a ruling granting partial success to the plaintiffs. Although the relevant patents have since expired, the litigation primarily addresses liability for past infringing acts and associated damages. This outcome underscores the strategic use of post-expiry litigation by originator companies to secure financial compensation and maintain market leverage even after patent protection formally ends.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，法国后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: Generic manufacturers entering the European market should assess the risk of retrospective infringement claims for activities conducted prior to patent expiration. Even after a patent lapses, originators may pursue significant damages for past unauthorized sales. Companies must conduct thorough freedom-to-operate analyses and maintain robust evidence databases for potential invalidity defenses. Monitoring judicial trends in key jurisdictions like France regarding the calculation of historical damages is essential for effective risk management and financial planning in the pharmaceutical sector.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Millennium指控仿制药企侵犯Velcade基础专利，巴黎法院裁定部分胜诉", "涉案专利保护硼酸化合物制剂，虽已过期但侵权追责仍在进行中", "案件凸显原研药企利用剩余法律手段延缓仿制药全面进入市场的策略"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Millennium and Cilag secured partial victory against generics for infringing Velcade formulation patents before their expiration.", "The case focuses on establishing liability and calculating damages for historical sales occurring while the patents were still in force.", "The ruling highlights how originators utilize remaining legal avenues to challenge generics and recover value after patent expiry."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕原研药企专利到期后的历史侵权追溯诉讼风险", "加强FTO分析与无效证据储备，应对潜在高额索赔"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Originators can still pursue substantial damages for pre-expiry sales, impacting generic launch economics and liability exposure.", "Practice note: Strengthen freedom-to-operate assessments and prepare comprehensive invalidity evidence to mitigate risks of retrospective high-value claims.", "What to monitor: Judicial approaches in France and other EU states toward quantifying damages for historical patent infringement post-expiry."]
core_points_en: Millennium and Cilag secured partial victory against generics for infringing Velcade formulation patents before their expiration.
The case focuses on establishing liability and calculating damages for historical sales occurring while the patents were still in force.
The ruling highlights how originators utilize remaining legal avenues to challenge generics and recover value after patent expiry.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Originators can still pursue substantial damages for pre-expiry sales, impacting generic launch economics and liability exposure.
Practice note: Strengthen freedom-to-operate assessments and prepare comprehensive invalidity evidence to mitigate risks of retrospective high-value claims.
What to monitor: Judicial approaches in France and other EU states toward quantifying damages for historical patent infringement post-expiry.

items.title: UPC revokes Chinese military medical research institute’s remdesivir patent
items.title_zh: UPC米兰分部撤销中国军科院瑞德西韦专利
items.title_en: UPC Milan Division Revokes Chinese Military Institute's Remdesivir Patent
items.summary: According to the judgment, the value of the patent dispute is €20 million. However, following the defeat, the Chinese Academy of Military Medical Sciences (AMMS) only has to pay €800,000, which it had previously agreed with Gilead. This week, the central division Milan completely revoked one of AMMS’ patents and rejected amendments (case ID: UPC_CFI_552/2025). […] The post UPC revokes Chinese military medical research institute’s remdesivir patent appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）米兰中央分部作出判决，完全撤销中国人民解放军军事医学科学院（AMMS）的一项瑞德西韦专利，并驳回其修改请求。本案争议标的额为2000万欧元，但依据此前与吉利德科学达成的协议，AMMS败诉后仅需支付80万欧元诉讼费用。该案件编号为UPC_CFI_552/2025，标志着中国科研机构在UPC体系下的专利维权遭遇重大挫折，凸显了欧洲专利无效程序的高风险性。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Central Division in Milan has issued a judgment completely revoking a remdesivir patent held by the Academy of Military Medical Sciences (AMMS) and rejecting its proposed amendments. The dispute carried a value of €20 million; however, pursuant to a prior agreement with Gilead Sciences, AMMS is liable for only €800,000 in litigation costs following this defeat. Identified as case UPC_CFI_552/2025, this ruling represents a significant setback for Chinese research institutions navigating the UPC system. It underscores the substantial risks inherent in European patent invalidation proceedings, where centralized jurisdiction can lead to immediate and widespread loss of patent rights across participating member states.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision illustrates the critical importance of rigorous pre-litigation stability assessments for patents enforced under the Unified Patent Court regime. Because UPC rulings have unitary effect, a successful invalidity action results in the immediate loss of patent rights across all designated contracting states. Rights holders should conduct thorough Freedom-to-Operate analyses before initiating infringement claims and prepare robust defenses against potential invalidity counterclaims. Additionally, parties may consider negotiating settlement agreements that cap financial exposure, thereby mitigating the severe economic consequences associated with centralized patent revocation procedures.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC米兰分部完全撤销中国军科院瑞德西韦专利并驳回修改", "争议标的2000万欧元，依协议败诉方仅付80万欧元诉讼费用", "案件编号UPC_CFI_552/2025，体现UPC对专利有效性严格审查"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UPC Milan fully revoked AMMS's remdesivir patent and rejected all proposed claim amendments.", "Dispute value was €20 million, but AMMS pays only €800,000 in costs per prior settlement.", "Case UPC_CFI_552/2025 highlights strict UPC scrutiny regarding patent validity standards."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC判决具全域效力，专利被撤将导致全境权利丧失", "建议在欧诉讼前加强FTO分析及预设无效抗辩策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: UPC judgments have unitary effect, meaning patent revocation can remove protection across all participating states where the unitary patent is in force.", "Practice note: Strengthen FTO analysis and prepare invalidity defense strategies before filing infringement suits.", "What to monitor: UPC trends in strict validity review and the enforceability of cost-capping settlement clauses."]
core_points_en: UPC Milan fully revoked AMMS's remdesivir patent and rejected all proposed claim amendments.
Dispute value was €20 million, but AMMS pays only €800,000 in costs per prior settlement.
Case UPC_CFI_552/2025 highlights strict UPC scrutiny regarding patent validity standards.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: UPC judgments have unitary effect, meaning patent revocation can remove protection across all participating states where the unitary patent is in force.
Practice note: Strengthen FTO analysis and prepare invalidity defense strategies before filing infringement suits.
What to monitor: UPC trends in strict validity review and the enforceability of cost-capping settlement clauses.

items.title: LD Hamburg, April 27, 2026, Decision, UPC_CFI_685/2024, UPC_CFI_157/2025
items.title_zh: UPC汉堡地院：辅助请求数量及权利要求宽泛解释影响新颖性
items.title_en: UPC Hamburg: High Auxiliary Requests Accepted; Broad Claim Interpretation Destroys Novelty
items.summary: The Local Division Hamburg of the Unified Patent Court examined infringement and revocation claims involving EP 2 077 811, a patent held by Teleflex Life Sciences II LLC and challenged by Speed Care Mineral GmbH. The proceedings addressed questions of patent validity under multiple European Patent Convention articles and the application of the Rules of Procedure in cross-border patent disputes across ten European jurisdictions.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）汉堡地方法院审理了Teleflex诉Speed Care案，涉及EP 2 077 811专利的侵权与无效。法院裁定在面临多重有效性挑战时，多达51项的辅助请求可被允许；但被告若已知理由却延迟提出反诉，将因缺乏勤勉而被驳回。此外，法院对“粘土材料”等术语进行宽泛的功能性解释，虽利于认定侵权，但也扩大了现有技术范围，导致专利新颖性丧失。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Local Division in Hamburg adjudicated infringement and revocation actions concerning EP 2 077 811, held by Teleflex Life Sciences II LLC against Speed Care Mineral GmbH. The Court permitted up to 51 auxiliary requests due to the complexity of multiple validity challenges. However, it rejected a late amendment to extend the counterclaim for revocation, citing the defendant’s lack of reasonable diligence under Rule 263(2)(a) RoP, as grounds were known months prior. Crucially, the Court applied a broad functional interpretation to terms like “clay material” and “binder.” While this approach potentially favored an infringement finding, it simultaneously expanded the scope of relevant prior art, ultimately leading to a finding that the patent lacked novelty.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC诉讼时，应注重程序策略的严谨性，避免以已知理由延迟抗辩。同时，需警惕权利要求宽泛解释带来的风险，在撰写和修改权利要求时平衡保护范围与新颖性，避免因功能性限定过宽而引入不利现有技术，确保专利稳定性。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision highlights critical procedural and substantive risks in UPC litigation. Parties must strictly adhere to procedural timelines and demonstrate reasonable diligence when raising validity defenses; delaying known arguments can lead to their exclusion. Substantively, while broad functional claim language may seem advantageous for establishing infringement, it carries significant risk. Such interpretations can inadvertently expand the pool of applicable prior art, undermining novelty. Practitioners should carefully balance claim breadth with stability, ensuring that functional definitions do not introduce unexpected anticipatory disclosures during validity assessments.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["面对复杂有效性挑战时，大量辅助请求可能被视为可接受", "被告延迟提出已知理由的反诉，因缺乏勤勉将被驳回", "宽泛的功能性权利要求解释可能扩大现有技术从而破坏新颖性"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Numerous auxiliary requests may be admissible when facing diverse and complex validity attacks regarding novelty and inventive step.", "Late amendments extending revocation counterclaims are rejected if the defendant lacks reasonable diligence under procedural rules.", "Broad functional claim interpretations can widen the prior art base, potentially destroying patent novelty despite aiding infringement."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC诉讼需严守程序时限，避免以已知理由延迟抗辩", "平衡权利要求范围与新颖性，警惕宽泛解释引入现有技术"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strict adherence to procedural deadlines is essential; delaying known validity arguments risks exclusion from proceedings.", "Practice note: Balance claim breadth with novelty stability; avoid overly broad functional definitions that may expand prior art scope.", "What to monitor: How courts interpret functional claim terms and their impact on the admissibility of prior art in validity challenges."]
core_points_en: Numerous auxiliary requests may be admissible when facing diverse and complex validity attacks regarding novelty and inventive step.
Late amendments extending revocation counterclaims are rejected if the defendant lacks reasonable diligence under procedural rules.
Broad functional claim interpretations can widen the prior art base, potentially destroying patent novelty despite aiding infringement.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strict adherence to procedural deadlines is essential; delaying known validity arguments risks exclusion from proceedings.
Practice note: Balance claim breadth with novelty stability; avoid overly broad functional definitions that may expand prior art scope.
What to monitor: How courts interpret functional claim terms and their impact on the admissibility of prior art in validity challenges.

items.title: Inspection order lifted after no main action was filed
items.title_zh: 未提起主诉讼，UPC杜塞尔多夫分院解除检查令
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf Lifts Inspection Order After Applicant Misses Deadline for Main Action
items.summary: OTEC v STEROS GPA INNOVATIVE — Local Division Düsseldorf, 4 May 2026 (UPC_CFI_885/2025)
items.ai_summary_zh: OTEC诉STEROS案中，杜塞尔多夫地方法院裁定，因申请人未在法定期限内就实质问题提起诉讼，必须解除单方证据保全与检查令。法院依据《统一专利法院协定》第60(8)条及规则198.1/199.2，责令返还、销毁所有获取的证据并不可逆删除数字副本。尽管被申请人请求赔偿，但因仅主张法律费用而未证明具体损害，法院驳回了补偿请求。此裁决明确了逾期不起诉的程序性后果。
items.ai_summary_en: In OTEC v STEROS GPA INNOVATIVE, the UPC Düsseldorf Local Division ruled that an ex parte inspection and preservation order must be lifted when the applicant fails to initiate proceedings on the merits within the statutory period. Citing Article 60(8) UPCA and Rules 198.1 and 199.2 RoP, the Court ordered the return of physical evidence, destruction of copies, and irreversible deletion of digital files obtained during the inspection. Although the respondent sought compensation, the Court rejected this claim because only legal costs were asserted without substantiated proof of specific damage. This judgment clarifies the strict procedural consequences of failing to file a main action after securing preliminary evidence measures under the Unified Patent Court framework.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: Practitioners must strictly monitor the 31-calendar-day or 20-working-day window to file a main action after evidence disclosure under the UPCA. Failure to comply triggers automatic lifting of orders and mandatory data destruction, risking loss of critical evidence. For respondents, this precedent confirms the right to demand immediate cessation of measures and secure deletion of sensitive information. However, securing financial redress requires demonstrating concrete harm beyond standard legal fees. Parties should prepare robust documentation of actual damages if seeking compensation for improperly executed or expired provisional measures.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["未在法定期限提起主诉讼，证据保全与检查令必须解除", "法院责令返还实物证据，销毁副本并不可逆删除数字文件", "仅主张法律费用而无具体损害证明，驳回补偿请求"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Inspection and preservation orders must be lifted if no main action is filed within the statutory deadline following disclosure.", "The Court ordered the return of physical evidence, destruction of copies, and irreversible deletion of all digital data obtained.", "Compensation claims based solely on legal costs are rejected absent proof of specific, substantiated actual damage."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["严守UPC证据保全后提起主诉讼的法定期限，防程序失效", "索赔需举证具体损害，单纯法律费用难获支持"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strict adherence to post-inspection filing deadlines is critical to prevent invalidation of evidence preservation efforts.", "Practice note: Respondents can effectively compel data deletion but must substantiate actual damages to recover compensation beyond legal costs.", "What to monitor: UPC divisions' consistent application of Rule 199.2 regarding the calculation of periods and evidentiary standards for damages."]
core_points_en: Inspection and preservation orders must be lifted if no main action is filed within the statutory deadline following disclosure.
The Court ordered the return of physical evidence, destruction of copies, and irreversible deletion of all digital data obtained.
Compensation claims based solely on legal costs are rejected absent proof of specific, substantiated actual damage.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strict adherence to post-inspection filing deadlines is critical to prevent invalidation of evidence preservation efforts.
Practice note: Respondents can effectively compel data deletion but must substantiate actual damages to recover compensation beyond legal costs.
What to monitor: UPC divisions' consistent application of Rule 199.2 regarding the calculation of periods and evidentiary standards for damages.

items.title: EU Returns to Special 301 Report’s Watch List for First Time Since 2006
items.title_zh: 欧盟自2006年来首次重返美国特别301报告观察名单
items.title_en: EU Returns to US Special 301 Watch List for First Time Since 2006
items.summary: On April 30, the Office of the U.S. Trade Representative (USTR) released this year’s Special 301 Report, which surveys the effectiveness of intellectual property (IP) rights and enforcement abroad and identifies foreign nations where IP protections are uncertain or disregarded. The 2026 report marks the first time in 13 years that a Priority Foreign Country (PFC) has been named, with Vietnam being identified as a PFC for persistent failures to address several long-standing IP concerns. The USTR
items.ai_summary_zh: 美国贸易代表办公室发布2026年特别301报告，将欧盟列入观察名单，系自2006年以来首次。报告指出越南因长期未解决IP问题被列为优先外国国家。此举标志着欧盟作为整体在知识产权保护方面受到美方关注，可能影响双边贸易关系及欧盟内部IP执法政策的调整方向。
items.ai_summary_en: On April 30, the Office of the U.S. Trade Representative released its 2026 Special 301 Report, assessing global intellectual property rights protection and enforcement effectiveness. For the first time since 2006, the European Union has been placed on the Watch List, signaling renewed US concerns regarding IP protections at the continental level. Additionally, Vietnam was designated as a Priority Foreign Country due to persistent failures to address longstanding IP issues. This development highlights significant scrutiny of EU-wide IP enforcement mechanisms, potentially influencing future bilateral trade relations and prompting adjustments in internal EU intellectual property policies and legislative frameworks.
items.ai_insight_zh: 欧盟被列入观察名单预示其IP执法政策可能趋严或面临审查。中国企业需密切关注欧盟后续立法动态及执法力度变化，特别是在跨境电商与数字版权领域。建议加强合规体系，提前应对潜在的贸易壁垒与IP纠纷风险，确保在欧业务稳健运行。
items.ai_insight_en: Inclusion on the Special 301 Watch List indicates heightened US scrutiny of EU intellectual property enforcement practices, suggesting potential regulatory tightening or increased review of current policies. Stakeholders should anticipate possible shifts in compliance expectations, particularly affecting cross-border digital commerce and copyright management. Companies operating within the EU jurisdiction are advised to strengthen their internal compliance frameworks and monitor legislative developments closely. Proactive adaptation to these evolving enforcement landscapes will be essential to mitigate risks associated with potential trade barriers and intellectual property disputes, ensuring continued operational stability and legal certainty in the European market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["美国USTR发布2026年特别301报告，欧盟首次自2006年后被列入观察名单", "越南因持续未能解决知识产权关切被指定为优先外国国家(PFC)", "报告评估全球IP保护有效性，欧盟整体执法状况引发美方贸易关切"]

items.title: Stop press: High Court hands down FRAND judgment in Samsung v ZTE
items.title_zh: 快讯：英国高等法院就三星诉中兴案作出FRAND判决
items.title_en: UK High Court Issues First Global Cross-Licence FRAND Judgment in Samsung v ZTE
items.summary: The High Court has today handed down its judgment in Samsung v ZTE, determining the FRAND terms of a global cross-licence between the parties. This is t...
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国高等法院就三星诉中兴案作出判决，确定双方全球交叉许可的FRAND条款。这是英国首例此类判决。法院详细指导了使用可比协议法确定FRAND条款的方法，特别是识别“良好可比协议”及调整非FRAND因素（如诉讼意愿和能力差异）。法院认为中兴因诉讼地位较弱而获得低价，现有权要求全额FRAND价值。判决主要基于中兴与苹果的可比许可协议。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK High Court has delivered its judgment in Samsung v ZTE, establishing the FRAND terms for a global cross-licence between the parties. This marks the first instance of such a determination reaching judgment in the United Kingdom. The decision offers detailed guidance on applying the comparable licences method, specifically regarding the identification of 'good comparables' and necessary adjustments for non-FRAND factors. The Court noted that ZTE’s perceived lower willingness or ability to litigate resulted in lower royalty rates in previous agreements with major licensees like Apple and Samsung. Consequently, the Court ruled that ZTE is entitled to full FRAND value, rejecting claims of hold-out by the licensors. The final determination relied primarily on the ZTE-Apple licence, which was deemed easier to unpack and adjust for differences in licensee status.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，英国后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment clarifies how courts may adjust FRAND rates based on disparities in litigation capability and willingness. Parties should proactively document evidence demonstrating their status as willing licensees to counter potential hold-out allegations. It is advisable to systematically review internal licensing agreements to quantify any historical disadvantages caused by unequal bargaining power or litigation resources. Such preparation helps ensure that future rate determinations reflect true market value rather than concessions made under duress. Monitoring how courts define and weigh these non-FRAND factors will be critical for developing robust negotiation strategies and defense positions in subsequent SEP disputes across European jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国高院首判全球交叉许可FRAND条款，确立可比协议法适用标准", "考量诉讼意愿与能力等非FRAND因素，认定中兴有权主张全额FRAND价值", "优先采用中兴与苹果的许可协议作为基准，因其更易于拆解分析"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["First UK judgment determining FRAND terms for a global cross-licence, setting precedents for comparable licence analysis.", "Adjusts royalties for non-FRAND factors like litigation capacity, affirming entitlement to full FRAND value despite past low rates.", "Prioritizes the ZTE-Apple agreement as the primary benchmark due to its relative ease of analysis and adjustment."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["强化善意被许可人证据，量化诉讼劣势对费率的影响", "梳理内部可比协议，应对英法院对非FRAND因素的审查"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Document willingness to license and quantify historical disadvantages from unequal litigation power.", "Practice note: Review internal comparable agreements to prepare for judicial scrutiny of non-FRAND adjustment factors.", "What to monitor: Judicial interpretation of litigation capacity impacts on FRAND rate calculations in future cases."]
core_points_en: First UK judgment determining FRAND terms for a global cross-licence, setting precedents for comparable licence analysis.
Adjusts royalties for non-FRAND factors like litigation capacity, affirming entitlement to full FRAND value despite past low rates.
Prioritizes the ZTE-Apple agreement as the primary benchmark due to its relative ease of analysis and adjustment.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Document willingness to license and quantify historical disadvantages from unequal litigation power.
Practice note: Review internal comparable agreements to prepare for judicial scrutiny of non-FRAND adjustment factors.
What to monitor: Judicial interpretation of litigation capacity impacts on FRAND rate calculations in future cases.

items.title: The UK: a world-class home for intellectual property
items.title_zh: 英国：世界级的知识产权家园
items.title_en: UK Intellectual Property Environment: A Global Leader in Trade Mark Protection and Legal Framework
items.summary: As newly appointed President of the Chartered Institute of Trade Mark Attorneys (CITMA), we were delighted when Daniel Hardman-Smart accepted our invitation to be one of our guest bloggers. In his blog, Daniel talks about the UK as a leading environment for intellectual property, particularly in relation to trade marks, and what
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章由特许商标律师协会新任主席撰写，强调英国作为全球知识产权保护枢纽的地位。引用美国商会国际IP指数，指出英国在商标领域并列第一。文章赞扬英国成熟的法律框架、专业的知识产权法院及高效的执法能力，认为其能为企业提供可靠的品牌保护。旨在向全球企业展示英国在商标注册、管理及维权方面的卓越环境，鼓励企业利用该体系保障品牌资产。
items.ai_summary_en: Authored by the newly appointed President of the Chartered Institute of Trade Mark Attorneys, this commentary underscores the United Kingdom’s status as a premier global hub for intellectual property protection. Citing the US Chamber of Commerce International IP Index, the article highlights that the UK ranks joint first worldwide for trade marks, reflecting its robust legal infrastructure. The piece praises the maturity of the UK’s legal framework, including respected specialist IP courts that deliver cost-effective and enforceable decisions. It emphasizes the international recognition of Chartered Trade Mark Attorneys for their commercial expertise. Ultimately, the blog aims to demonstrate to global businesses how the UK’s efficient and reliable system supports brand registration, management, and enforcement, ensuring comprehensive protection for valuable brand assets.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出口企业应重视英国商标布局，利用其高效司法体系维权。鉴于英国IP环境优越，建议企业在英建立完整商标组合，并借助当地专业律师资源，确保品牌在英及国际市场的合规与安全，降低侵权风险。
items.ai_insight_en: For companies operating in or entering the UK market, leveraging the jurisdiction’s high-ranking IP framework is critical for securing brand assets. The combination of specialized courts and expert legal practitioners offers a reliable pathway for both registration and enforcement. Businesses should consider establishing comprehensive trade mark portfolios within the UK to mitigate infringement risks effectively. Utilizing local professional resources ensures compliance with sophisticated legal standards, enhancing overall brand security. This environment provides a strategic advantage for managing international IP rights, offering efficient resolution mechanisms and strong legal protections that command respect globally, thereby supporting sustainable business growth and risk management strategies.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国在国际IP指数中商标保护得分并列第一，法律环境成熟", "拥有受尊重的专业知识产权法院，提供高效且具执行力的裁决", "特许商标律师具备国际认可的专业知识，助力企业品牌全球化"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UK achieves a joint first ranking globally for trade mark protection in the latest US Chamber of Commerce International IP Index.", "Specialist intellectual property courts provide mature, sophisticated, and enforceable legal decisions recognized across multiple jurisdictions.", "Chartered Trade Mark Attorneys offer internationally recognized expertise and commercial judgment to support effective brand globalization."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用英国高效司法体系强化品牌海外维权能力", "借助当地专业资源完善商标组合以规避国际风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage the UK's top-tier trade mark ranking and specialized courts to secure robust brand protection and enforce rights efficiently against infringers.", "Practice note: Engage Chartered Trade Mark Attorneys to build comprehensive local portfolios, ensuring alignment with sophisticated legal standards and commercial best practices.", "What to monitor: Track developments in UK IP enforcement mechanisms and court rulings to maintain competitive advantage and minimize international infringement risks."]
core_points_en: The UK achieves a joint first ranking globally for trade mark protection in the latest US Chamber of Commerce International IP Index.
Specialist intellectual property courts provide mature, sophisticated, and enforceable legal decisions recognized across multiple jurisdictions.
Chartered Trade Mark Attorneys offer internationally recognized expertise and commercial judgment to support effective brand globalization.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage the UK's top-tier trade mark ranking and specialized courts to secure robust brand protection and enforce rights efficiently against infringers.
Practice note: Engage Chartered Trade Mark Attorneys to build comprehensive local portfolios, ensuring alignment with sophisticated legal standards and commercial best practices.
What to monitor: Track developments in UK IP enforcement mechanisms and court rulings to maintain competitive advantage and minimize international infringement risks.
