items.title: Le processus d'innovation et les réponses PI
items.title_zh: 法国INPI解析创新全流程中的知识产权应对策略
items.title_en: INPI Guidance: Integrating IP Strategies Across the Six Stages of Innovation
items.summary: Le processus d'innovation et les réponses PI llegrand@inpi.fr 30 avril 2026 La lettre d'information Texte Cette infographie fait partie des contenus envoyés dans la newsletter Coaching INPI. Cette newsletter a pour objectif de vous informer tous les mois sur des sujets de propriété industrielle. Inscrivez-vous © Items Description détaillée de l'infographie IDÉE Étape 1 — Chercher / Sélectionner Comment trouver des opportunités d'innovation et créer un nouveau produit ? Veille / Étude liberté d'e
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国工业产权局（INPI）在“Coaching INPI”通讯中发布信息图，详解从创意到产业化的六个创新阶段及其对应的知识产权策略。内容涵盖自由实施检索、法国专利申请、概念验证合同、商业化扩展、产品审查及最终的价值评估。文章提供了各阶段的典型成本区间（如申请费4-6千欧元），旨在指导企业将IP融入创新全生命周期，优化预算分配并构建国际保护组合。
items.ai_summary_en: The French National Institute of Industrial Property (INPI) released an infographic within its 'Coaching INPI' newsletter, detailing intellectual property strategies aligned with six distinct stages of the innovation lifecycle. The guidance maps specific IP actions to phases ranging from initial idea selection and R&D design to commercial exploitation and final asset valuation. Key measures include freedom-to-operate searches, French patent filings, contractual frameworks for concept validation, and strategies for international title extension. The document provides indicative cost ranges for each phase, such as €4,000–€6,000 for French applications, based on a portfolio including at least one trademark and one patent extended internationally. This resource aims to assist enterprises in integrating IP management into their innovation processes, optimizing budget allocation, and constructing robust international protection portfolios.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国企业应重视早期FTO检索以规避侵权风险，并利用法国申请作为进入欧洲市场的低成本跳板。建议结合INPI提供的成本模型，合理规划从单一国家申请到国际布局的资金节奏，强化合同管理与专利资产估值能力，提升整体IP运营效率。
items.ai_insight_en: This INPI framework offers a structured approach for aligning IP expenditures with innovation milestones. For entities operating in Europe, early freedom-to-operate analysis remains critical to mitigate infringement risks during product development. Utilizing the French filing system as a cost-effective entry point can facilitate broader European market access. Companies should integrate these cost models into their financial planning to manage the transition from national filings to international protection. Furthermore, strengthening contractual governance during the concept validation phase and enhancing patent valuation capabilities are essential for maximizing the commercial return on IP investments and ensuring sustainable competitive advantage.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["梳理创新六阶段：从创意筛选、研发设计到商业化及价值评估的完整路径", "明确各阶段IP动作：包括FTO检索、法国专利申请及后续的国际权利延伸策略", "提供成本参考：给出各阶段平均费用区间，助力企业精准规划IP预算与资源"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Outlines a comprehensive six-stage innovation pathway, covering activities from initial idea screening and research design through to commercialization and value assessment.", "Defines specific IP actions for each stage, emphasizing freedom-to-operate searches, French patent applications, and subsequent strategies for extending rights internationally.", "Provides indicative cost benchmarks for each phase to assist companies in precise IP budget planning and efficient resource allocation across the product lifecycle."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用法国申请低成本优势，构建进入欧洲市场的初步专利护城河", "强化早期自由实施检索与合同管理，降低出海过程中的法律与财务风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage the cost efficiency of French patent applications to establish an initial protective barrier for entering the European market.", "Practice note: Prioritize early freedom-to-operate searches and robust contractual management to reduce legal and financial exposure during international expansion.", "What to monitor: Track updates to INPI’s cost estimation models and adjust international IP budgeting rhythms accordingly to optimize portfolio growth."]
core_points_en: Outlines a comprehensive six-stage innovation pathway, covering activities from initial idea screening and research design through to commercialization and value assessment.
Defines specific IP actions for each stage, emphasizing freedom-to-operate searches, French patent applications, and subsequent strategies for extending rights internationally.
Provides indicative cost benchmarks for each phase to assist companies in precise IP budget planning and efficient resource allocation across the product lifecycle.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage the cost efficiency of French patent applications to establish an initial protective barrier for entering the European market.
Practice note: Prioritize early freedom-to-operate searches and robust contractual management to reduce legal and financial exposure during international expansion.
What to monitor: Track updates to INPI’s cost estimation models and adjust international IP budgeting rhythms accordingly to optimize portfolio growth.

items.title: Fermeture de l’INPI jeudi 14 et vendredi 15 mai 2026
items.title_zh: 法国INPI2026年5月14-15日暂停工业产权服务
items.title_en: INPI Suspends Industrial Property Services on May 14–15, 2026
items.summary: Fermeture de l’INPI jeudi 14 et vendredi 15 mai 2026 llegrand@inpi.fr 30 avril 2026 La prorogation de délai au jour ouvrable suivant pour les jours fériés ou chômés est prévue aux articles R. 514-2, R. 618-3 et R. 718-2 du Code de la PI. Si vous rencontrez des difficultés d'accès à l'espace e-procédures ou un problème technique , vous pouvez nous contacter au +33 (0)1 56 65 89 98. Décision n° 2025-138 du 21 octobre 2025 relative aux journées de fermeture de l'INPI en 2026.pdf 59.76 Ko Titre À no
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国国家工业产权局（INPI）宣布，因节假日安排，将于2026年5月14日（周四）和15日（周五）暂停提供工业产权相关服务。根据《知识产权法典》相关规定，若法定期限届满日为非工作日，期限将顺延至下一个工作日。在此期间，INPI Direct信息服务针对商标、专利及外观设计等工业产权咨询将关闭，但企业行政手续服务仍正常开放。如遇技术故障，用户可通过指定电话联系支持。
items.ai_summary_en: The French National Institute of Industrial Property (INPI) announces the suspension of industrial property services on Thursday, May 14, and Friday, May 15, 2026, due to holiday arrangements. According to Articles R. 514-2, R. 618-3, and R. 718-2 of the Intellectual Property Code, statutory deadlines falling on non-working days are automatically extended to the next working day. During this period, INPI Direct information services regarding trademarks, patents, and designs will be closed. However, services related to corporate formalities, such as business creation, modifications, cessation, and annual account filings, will remain open from 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM. Users experiencing technical issues with the e-procedures portal may contact support at +33 (0)1 56 65 89 98.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国企业需特别注意2026年5月中旬的期限顺延风险。若关键IP申请或答复期限恰逢该周末，务必提前规划提交，避免依赖顺延机制导致延误。建议监控INPI官方日历，确保在截止日前完成所有工业产权程序，保障权利稳定性。
items.ai_insight_en: Practitioners should carefully assess filing strategies around mid-May 2026, noting that while deadlines expiring on these dates extend to the following Monday, reliance on this mechanism carries procedural risks. It is advisable to complete critical IP applications and responses before the holiday weekend to ensure certainty and avoid potential delays associated with post-holiday processing volumes. Monitoring the official INPI calendar remains essential for maintaining compliance and protecting rights stability across European jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["INPI于2026年5月14日至15日暂停工业产权咨询服务", "期限届满日为节假日时依法顺延至下一工作日", "企业设立与年报等行政手续服务期间保持正常开放"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["INPI suspends industrial property consultations for trademarks, patents, and designs on May 14–15, 2026.", "Statutory deadlines expiring on these holidays are legally extended to the subsequent working day.", "Corporate administrative services remain operational during the closure for standard business hours."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注期限顺延规则，避免因节假日导致IP程序延误", "提前规划五月中旬的关键申请与答复，确保合规"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Verify if any critical IP deadlines fall on May 14–15, 2026, to leverage automatic extensions safely.", "Practice note: Prioritize early submission of key applications and responses before mid-May to mitigate procedural risks.", "What to monitor: Official INPI announcements for any changes to service availability or technical support protocols."]
core_points_en: INPI suspends industrial property consultations for trademarks, patents, and designs on May 14–15, 2026.
Statutory deadlines expiring on these holidays are legally extended to the subsequent working day.
Corporate administrative services remain operational during the closure for standard business hours.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Verify if any critical IP deadlines fall on May 14–15, 2026, to leverage automatic extensions safely.
Practice note: Prioritize early submission of key applications and responses before mid-May to mitigate procedural risks.
What to monitor: Official INPI announcements for any changes to service availability or technical support protocols.

items.title: Mirror, mirror on the wall — Who is the most distinctive of them all?
items.title_zh: 镜中谁最具显著性？CJEU将裁定写实肖像商标注册资格
items.title_en: CJEU to Rule on Inherent Distinctiveness of Photorealistic Portrait Trademarks in Case C-3/26
items.summary: The question whether photorealistic portraits of people’s faces are inherently distinctive and can be registered as trade marks has been the subject of some debate (IPKat here, here and here). Now the Court of Justice of the EU (“CJEU”) gets to weigh in in a reference for a preliminary ruling from the Budapest High Court in Dr. Czirják-Nagy Ügyvédi Iroda (case C-3/26).   Background On 12 February
items.ai_summary_zh: 布达佩斯高等法院就Dr. Czirják-Nagy Ügyvédi Iroda案（C-3/26）向欧盟法院提交初步裁决请求。核心争议在于人物面部照片写实肖像是否具备固有显著性并可注册为商标。此前该问题在业界引发广泛辩论，此次CJEU的介入将为欧盟范围内此类非传统标志的可注册性及显著性判断提供关键法律指引。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) has received a request for a preliminary ruling from the Budapest High Court regarding the trademark eligibility of photorealistic facial portraits. The reference, filed in the case of Dr. Czirják-Nagy Ügyvédi Iroda (Case C-3/26), addresses whether such realistic images possess inherent distinctiveness under EU trademark law. This issue has sparked significant debate within the intellectual property community, particularly concerning non-traditional marks. The CJEU’s intervention is expected to provide critical legal guidance on the registrability and distinctiveness assessment of these visual elements across the European Union. The outcome will clarify the standards for registering portrait-based trademarks, impacting brand protection strategies for entities relying on human imagery.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业若计划将品牌代言人或创始人肖像注册为欧盟商标，需密切关注此案进展。若CJEU认定缺乏固有显著性，企业需提前准备通过使用获得显著性的证据。建议优化品牌布局策略，避免过度依赖单一肖像注册，结合文字图形组合申请以增强保护稳定性。
items.ai_insight_en: Stakeholders utilizing personal likenesses, such as founders or brand ambassadors, should closely monitor this proceeding. If the CJEU determines that photorealistic portraits lack inherent distinctiveness, applicants must be prepared to demonstrate acquired distinctiveness through extensive market use. Relying solely on portrait registrations may pose risks; therefore, diversifying brand portfolios with word or combined marks is advisable to ensure robust protection. Legal teams should evaluate current applications for potential vulnerabilities and consider gathering evidence of consumer recognition proactively. This case highlights the importance of strategic planning when securing rights for non-traditional visual identifiers in the European market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["布达佩斯高等法院提请CJEU对写实肖像固有显著性作出初步裁决", "案件核心争议为人物面部照片写实肖像能否注册为欧盟商标", "CJEU裁决将明确此类非传统标志在欧盟的注册标准与法律界限"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Budapest High Court refers the question of inherent distinctiveness for photorealistic portraits to the CJEU for a preliminary ruling.", "The core legal dispute centers on whether realistic photographs of human faces qualify for registration as EU trademarks without acquired distinctiveness.", "The forthcoming CJEU decision will establish definitive registration criteria and legal boundaries for portrait-based non-traditional marks in the EU."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注CJEU裁决，评估肖像商标注册风险及固有显著性要求", "提前收集使用证据，探索通过市场使用获得显著性的合规路径"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess risks associated with registering founder or ambassador portraits if inherent distinctiveness is denied by the CJEU.", "Practice note: Prepare evidence of acquired distinctiveness through use and consider combining portraits with text for stronger protection.", "What to monitor: Track the CJEU's final judgment to adjust EU trademark filing strategies for non-traditional portrait marks accordingly."]
core_points_en: The Budapest High Court refers the question of inherent distinctiveness for photorealistic portraits to the CJEU for a preliminary ruling.
The core legal dispute centers on whether realistic photographs of human faces qualify for registration as EU trademarks without acquired distinctiveness.
The forthcoming CJEU decision will establish definitive registration criteria and legal boundaries for portrait-based non-traditional marks in the EU.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess risks associated with registering founder or ambassador portraits if inherent distinctiveness is denied by the CJEU.
Practice note: Prepare evidence of acquired distinctiveness through use and consider combining portraits with text for stronger protection.
What to monitor: Track the CJEU's final judgment to adjust EU trademark filing strategies for non-traditional portrait marks accordingly.

items.title: Confidentiality ambush at the UPC: disclose once, lose it forever (CoA on Rule 262.2 RoP), (UPC_CoA_930/2025)
items.title_zh: UPC裁定：未获保护令披露即失密，保密 ambush 风险警示
items.title_en: UPC Ruling: Disclosure Without Protective Order Irreversibly Waives Trade Secret Status
items.summary: Court docket: Court of Appeal
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC上诉法院在EOFlow诉Insulet案中裁定，若未在披露前依据程序规则第262A条获得保护令或明确限制，向对方披露的信息将永久丧失商业秘密地位。法院区分了限制公众查阅（262.2条）与限制对方使用（262A条），并驳回了“仅限诉讼目的”的隐含限制主张。此举强调程序合规是维持秘密性的必要步骤，未采取合理保密措施将导致信息性质不可逆地改变。
items.ai_summary_en: In EOFlow v. Insulet, the UPC Court of Appeal ruled that disclosing business information to an opposing party without a prior protective order under Rule 262A RoP results in the permanent loss of trade secret status. The Court strictly distinguished between restricting public access to court files under Rule 262.2 and restricting the opposing party’s use or disclosure under Rule 262A. It explicitly rejected the argument that an implicit procedural limitation exists, confining the use of disclosed information solely to litigation purposes. Consequently, failing to secure express confidentiality restrictions means reasonable steps to maintain secrecy were not taken. This decision underscores that procedural compliance is essential for preserving confidentiality, as unregulated disclosure fundamentally alters the legal nature of the information, rendering subsequent protection requests futile.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在UPC诉讼中需极度警惕“保密伏击”。在提交任何敏感财务或合同数据前，必须同步申请第262A条保护令，切勿依赖默示保密义务。应建立早期识别机制，评估披露对平行诉讼的影响，必要时引入“无溢出规则”，避免因程序疏忽导致核心商业机密在欧洲市场彻底公开且无法挽回。
items.ai_insight_en: Litigants must proactively secure Rule 262A protective orders before exchanging sensitive financial or contractual data, rather than relying on assumed confidentiality. The absence of explicit restrictions constitutes a waiver of trade secret protections, creating significant risks for parallel proceedings and broader market exposure. Legal teams should implement early identification protocols for confidential materials and assess potential spill-over effects in concurrent national or international disputes. Establishing clear 'no-spill' mechanisms and ensuring strict adherence to procedural requirements are critical to preventing the inadvertent public disclosure of core commercial secrets within the UPC framework.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["未依262A条获保护令即披露，信息将永久丧失商业秘密法律地位", "严格区分限制公众查阅与限制对方使用，前者不自动约束后者", "驳回诉讼用途隐含限制论，缺乏程序限制视为未采取合理保密措施"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Disclosure without a Rule 262A protective order causes irreversible loss of trade secret legal status.", "Rule 262.2 restricts public file access but does not automatically impose confidentiality obligations on the opposing party.", "The Court rejects implicit litigation-purpose limitations, treating lack of procedural restrictions as failure to take reasonable secrecy steps."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["提交敏感证据前务必同步申请262A条保护令，杜绝裸奔披露", "摒弃默示保密幻想，建立早期识别与平行诉讼溢出风险评估机制"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Secure Rule 262A protective orders prior to any sensitive data exchange to prevent irreversible loss of trade secret status.", "Practice note: Abandon reliance on implicit confidentiality; implement early identification mechanisms for confidential evidence and parallel litigation risk assessment.", "What to monitor: Strict procedural compliance regarding confidentiality undertakings to avoid inadvertent public disclosure of core commercial secrets."]
core_points_en: Disclosure without a Rule 262A protective order causes irreversible loss of trade secret legal status.
Rule 262.2 restricts public file access but does not automatically impose confidentiality obligations on the opposing party.
The Court rejects implicit litigation-purpose limitations, treating lack of procedural restrictions as failure to take reasonable secrecy steps.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Secure Rule 262A protective orders prior to any sensitive data exchange to prevent irreversible loss of trade secret status.
Practice note: Abandon reliance on implicit confidentiality; implement early identification mechanisms for confidential evidence and parallel litigation risk assessment.
What to monitor: Strict procedural compliance regarding confidentiality undertakings to avoid inadvertent public disclosure of core commercial secrets.

items.title: Official Statistics: Facts and figures: patents, trade marks, designs and hearings 2025
items.title_zh: 英国知识产权局发布2025年专利商标及外观设计官方统计
items.title_en: UKIPO Releases 2025 Official Statistics on Patents, Trade Marks, and Designs
items.summary: Official statistics include the latest annual data breakdowns for patents, trade marks, and designs for 2025, based on IPO administrative data.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局（UKIPO）发布了基于行政数据的2025年年度官方统计数据。该报告详细拆解了专利、商标和外观设计的最新年度数据分布，涵盖申请量、注册量及相关听证会情况。作为英国知识产权体系的核心情报，这些数据反映了当前英国市场的创新活跃度与知识产权保护现状，为权利人评估市场策略提供了量化依据。
items.ai_summary_en: The United Kingdom Intellectual Property Office (UKIPO) has published its official annual statistics for 2025, derived from internal administrative records. This comprehensive report provides detailed breakdowns of activity across patents, trade marks, and registered designs. The data covers the entire lifecycle from initial applications to final registrations, offering a granular view of filing volumes and grant rates. Additionally, the publication includes statistics on hearings and appeals, reflecting dispute resolution trends within the UK system. As a core intelligence source for the British IP landscape, these figures illustrate current innovation dynamics and protection levels. Stakeholders can utilize this quantitative evidence to assess market competitiveness, track sector-specific trends, and evaluate the overall health of intellectual property enforcement and registration processes in the United Kingdom.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，英国后续是否会围绕这类数据发布给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This dataset offers critical benchmarks for evaluating innovation intensity and competitive positioning within the UK market. By analyzing year-on-year trends in specific technology sectors, rights holders can identify emerging hotspots or signs of market saturation. Comparing current filing patterns against historical baselines allows for more accurate forecasting of competitor strategies and potential congestion points. Organizations should integrate these insights into their broader international portfolio management frameworks. Adjusting resource allocation based on these empirical trends helps optimize filing efficiency and ensures robust IP coverage in key business areas, supporting strategic decision-making for market entry and expansion.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UKIPO发布2025年专利、商标及外观设计年度官方统计数据", "数据基于UKIPO内部行政记录，涵盖申请至注册全流程指标", "提供各知识产权类别的详细年度分解数据及听证会概况"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UKIPO publishes 2025 annual official statistics covering patents, trade marks, and designs based on administrative data.", "Data encompasses full-process metrics from initial application through to final registration for all major IP categories.", "Report provides detailed annual breakdowns by IP type and includes an overview of hearing and appeal activities."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用UKIPO数据监测英国特定技术领域申请热度以预判竞争态势", "参考年度统计优化中国企业在英专利申请策略与资源分配效率"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Use UKIPO data to monitor application heat in specific technical fields for anticipating competitive landscapes.", "Practice note: Reference annual statistics to optimize overseas patent application strategies and improve resource allocation efficiency.", "What to monitor: Track shifts in filing volumes and hearing outcomes to adjust portfolio management and compliance tactics."]
core_points_en: UKIPO publishes 2025 annual official statistics covering patents, trade marks, and designs based on administrative data.
Data encompasses full-process metrics from initial application through to final registration for all major IP categories.
Report provides detailed annual breakdowns by IP type and includes an overview of hearing and appeal activities.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Use UKIPO data to monitor application heat in specific technical fields for anticipating competitive landscapes.
Practice note: Reference annual statistics to optimize overseas patent application strategies and improve resource allocation efficiency.
What to monitor: Track shifts in filing volumes and hearing outcomes to adjust portfolio management and compliance tactics.

items.title: Canfield now faces injunction in five European countries in QuantifiCare dispute
items.title_zh: Canfield在QuantifiCare纠纷中面临五国UPC禁令
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf Injuncts Canfield in Five Countries Over QuantifiCare Patent Dispute
items.summary: Canfield may no longer sell its 3D imaging system Canfield Vectra H2 in Belgium, France, Italy, and the Netherlands. Last week the Düsseldorf local division under presiding judge Ronny Thomas ruled that Canfield had infringed QuantifiCare’s patent EP 3 156 843. The UPC judges also rejected Canfield’s counterclaim for revocation (case IDs: UPC_CFI_559/2024 and UPC_CFI_106/2025). […] The post Canfield now faces injunction in five European countries in QuantifiCare dispute appeared first on JUVE Pa
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）杜塞尔多夫地方法庭裁定，Canfield侵犯QuantifiCare专利EP 3 156 843，并驳回其撤销请求。此举导致Canfield的3D成像系统Vectra H2在比利时、法国、意大利和荷兰面临销售禁令，加上德国共涉及五国。该判决凸显UPC在快速解决跨国专利争议中的效力，对涉案产品的欧洲市场流通构成直接阻碍。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s (UPC) Düsseldorf local division has ruled that Canfield infringed QuantifiCare’s patent EP 3 156 843. Presiding Judge Ronny Thomas issued an injunction prohibiting the sale of Canfield’s Vectra H2 3D imaging system across Belgium, France, Italy, and the Netherlands. This ruling adds to existing restrictions in Germany, effectively blocking sales in five European countries simultaneously. The court also rejected Canfield’s counterclaim seeking revocation of the patent, thereby upholding its validity. This decision underscores the UPC’s capacity to deliver rapid, cross-border enforcement outcomes. For market participants, the judgment highlights how a single UPC proceeding can create immediate, widespread barriers to trade, significantly disrupting product distribution and commercial operations across multiple major European jurisdictions at once.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This case illustrates the strategic impact of the UPC’s centralized jurisdiction, where a single infringement finding triggers immediate, multi-jurisdictional market exclusion. Rights holders gain significant leverage through efficient cross-border enforcement, while alleged infringers face heightened risks of simultaneous sales bans across key European markets. Companies operating in Europe must prioritize robust Freedom to Operate (FTO) analyses before product launches to mitigate these exposure risks. Furthermore, establishing rapid response protocols for potential litigation is critical. Defenders should prepare comprehensive invalidity evidence chains early to challenge patent validity swiftly, ensuring they can negotiate settlements or appeal effectively without suffering prolonged, continent-wide commercial disruption.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC杜塞尔多夫法庭认定Canfield侵犯QuantifiCare专利权", "法院驳回Canfield撤销专利的反诉，维持专利有效性", "禁令覆盖比法意荷四国，叠加德国共五国禁止销售涉案产品"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Düsseldorf local division found Canfield liable for infringing QuantifiCare’s patent EP 3 156 843 regarding its 3D imaging technology.", "Judges dismissed Canfield’s counterclaim for patent revocation, confirming the validity and enforceability of QuantifiCare’s intellectual property rights.", "The resulting injunction bans sales of the Vectra H2 system in Belgium, France, Italy, and the Netherlands, alongside Germany."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重视UPC跨国禁令效力，提前布局专利自由实施分析", "建立快速响应机制，准备专利无效证据以应对突发诉讼"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess exposure to UPC's pan-European injunction powers which can halt sales in multiple jurisdictions simultaneously upon a single adverse ruling.", "Practice note: Conduct thorough pre-launch FTO analyses and prepare robust invalidity evidence chains to defend against sudden cross-border patent assertions.", "What to monitor: Track UPC procedural developments and settlement trends, as rapid enforcement may necessitate quick strategic pivots to preserve market access."]
core_points_en: The UPC Düsseldorf local division found Canfield liable for infringing QuantifiCare’s patent EP 3 156 843 regarding its 3D imaging technology.
Judges dismissed Canfield’s counterclaim for patent revocation, confirming the validity and enforceability of QuantifiCare’s intellectual property rights.
The resulting injunction bans sales of the Vectra H2 system in Belgium, France, Italy, and the Netherlands, alongside Germany.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess exposure to UPC's pan-European injunction powers which can halt sales in multiple jurisdictions simultaneously upon a single adverse ruling.
Practice note: Conduct thorough pre-launch FTO analyses and prepare robust invalidity evidence chains to defend against sudden cross-border patent assertions.
What to monitor: Track UPC procedural developments and settlement trends, as rapid enforcement may necessitate quick strategic pivots to preserve market access.

items.title: All you need is dialogue: the CJEU interprets ‘pastiche’ in Pelham II
items.title_zh: CJEU在Pelham II案中界定“拼贴画”例外
items.title_en: CJEU Defines Pastiche Exception in Pelham II: Artistic Dialogue and Objective Recognition
items.summary: On 14 April 2026, the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) delivered its judgment in Pelham II, bringing partial closure to litigation spanning more than two decades.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年4月14日，欧盟法院就Pelham II案作出判决，历时二十余年的Kraftwerk采样纠纷获部分终结。法院澄清《信息社会指令》第5(3)(k)条“拼贴画”例外非万能条款，要求作品需唤起原作但保持明显差异，并进行可识别的艺术对话。判定不依赖主观意图，而看客观可识别性。此裁决平衡了版权独占与创作自由，为采样艺术家提供确定性但也设限。
items.ai_summary_en: On 14 April 2026, the Court of Justice of the European Union delivered its judgment in Pelham II, partially resolving litigation spanning over two decades regarding unauthorized sampling. The dispute concerned a two-second sample from Kraftwerk’s Metall auf Metall used in Sabrina Setlur’s track. The Court clarified that the “pastiche” exception under Article 5(3)(k) of the InfoSoc Directive is not a catch-all provision for all artistic reuse. Instead, it requires works to evoke existing works while remaining perceptibly different, engaging in a recognizable artistic dialogue. The ruling emphasizes objective recognizability by an audience familiar with the original work, rather than the user’s subjective intention. This decision balances exclusive copyright rights with creative freedom, offering legal certainty for sampling artists while establishing clear constraints on invoking the pastiche defense.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国音乐及数字内容企业在欧运营时，使用采样需谨慎评估是否构成“拼贴画”。建议确保新作品与原作好区分并体现艺术对话，避免单纯复制。建立合规审查机制，关注客观可识别性标准，以降低侵权风险并利用例外条款保护创作自由。
items.ai_insight_en: For entities operating in Europe, this judgment refines the boundaries of the pastiche exception for sampling and artistic reuse. Practitioners should assess whether new works maintain perceptible differences from originals and facilitate a recognizable artistic dialogue, avoiding mere replication. Compliance strategies must prioritize objective recognizability standards, ensuring that borrowed elements are identifiable to audiences familiar with the source material. Establishing robust internal review mechanisms can help mitigate infringement risks while leveraging the exception to protect creative freedom. Monitoring future national court applications will be essential to understand how these constraints impact practical enforcement and licensing negotiations in the digital content sector.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["拒绝将“拼贴画”作宽泛兜底解释，明确其不适用于所有艺术再利用形式", "定义核心特征为模仿风格且与原作好区分，旨在进行可识别的艺术对话", "判定标准侧重客观可识别性而非用户主观意图，需熟悉原作的公众能辨识"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Court rejected a broad interpretation, clarifying that the pastiche exception does not cover all forms of artistic engagement with protected works.", "Pastiche requires evoking existing works while remaining perceptibly different, aiming to engage in a recognizable artistic or creative dialogue.", "Classification depends on objective recognizability to those familiar with the original work, not on the subjective intention of the user."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["采样需确保与原作好区分并进行艺术对话，避免单纯复制以符合例外", "关注客观可识别性标准，建立合规审查机制以降低在欧版权侵权风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure sampled works remain perceptibly distinct from originals and engage in recognizable artistic dialogue to qualify for the pastiche exception.", "Practice note: Focus compliance reviews on objective recognizability standards rather than subjective user intent to reduce infringement liability.", "What to monitor: Track subsequent national court interpretations to gauge how these constraints affect enforcement and licensing practices."]
core_points_en: The Court rejected a broad interpretation, clarifying that the pastiche exception does not cover all forms of artistic engagement with protected works.
Pastiche requires evoking existing works while remaining perceptibly different, aiming to engage in a recognizable artistic or creative dialogue.
Classification depends on objective recognizability to those familiar with the original work, not on the subjective intention of the user.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure sampled works remain perceptibly distinct from originals and engage in recognizable artistic dialogue to qualify for the pastiche exception.
Practice note: Focus compliance reviews on objective recognizability standards rather than subjective user intent to reduce infringement liability.
What to monitor: Track subsequent national court interpretations to gauge how these constraints affect enforcement and licensing practices.

items.title: UPC: Supplementing Expert Opinion After Inspection Not Possible
items.title_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫分部裁定：检查后不得补充专家意见
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf Denies Supplementary Expert Opinion Following Ex Parte Evidence Preservation
items.summary: The Düsseldorf local division of the Unified Patent Court has ruled on a defendant's request for a supplementary expert opinion following an ex-parte inspection order.
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫地方法院在UPC_CFI_1325/2025案中裁定，驳回被告要求法院委任专家就单方证据保全出具补充意见的请求。法院指出，被告可通过《程序规则》第197.3条申请审查以保障程序权利，且该专家非第187条规定的“法院专家”，无法律依据支持补充报告。证据保全旨在快速获取信息以决定是否起诉，实质准确性异议应在后续主程序中提出。
items.ai_summary_en: The Düsseldorf Local Division of the Unified Patent Court dismissed a defendant’s request for a supplementary expert opinion following an ex parte inspection order in case UPC_CFI_1325/2025. The court held that no legal basis existed for such an order, distinguishing the court-commissioned inspector from a formal 'court expert' under Rule 187 of the Rules of Procedure. Consequently, parties lack the right to comment on the initial report. The panel emphasized that evidence preservation aims to rapidly gather information to decide on litigation, with substantive accuracy challenges reserved for main proceedings. Procedural safeguards were deemed sufficient via Rule 197.3 review applications. While rejecting the supplementary report, the court noted specific technical queries regarding the expert’s sketches and access to protective letters, signaling these issues will likely resurface during subsequent main actions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧遭遇单方证据保全时，应聚焦于利用Rule 197.3申请审查而非纠缠补充报告。需重视保全阶段的策略应对，因实质抗辩将延至主程序。建议提前准备技术对比分析，确保在主程序中有效反驳专家初步结论，避免程序被动。
items.ai_insight_en: Parties subject to ex parte evidence preservation should prioritize procedural reviews under Rule 197.3 rather than seeking supplementary reports, which are unlikely to succeed. Since substantive technical disputes are deferred to main proceedings, immediate strategy should focus on preserving rights for later argumentation. Legal teams must meticulously prepare detailed technical counter-analyses and ensure all relevant documentation, such as protective letters, is formally recorded. This approach ensures effective rebuttal of preliminary expert findings when the case moves to the substantive phase, avoiding procedural delays while maintaining strong defensive positions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["驳回单方保全后补充专家意见请求，明确缺乏法律依据", "区分法院委任专家与法定法院专家，排除当事人评论权", "保全程序侧重效率，实体争议留待主程序解决"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Request for supplementary expert opinion after ex parte inspection denied due to lack of legal basis.", "Distinction made between court-commissioned inspectors and formal court experts under Rule 187 RoP.", "Substantive objections to evidence accuracy must be raised in main proceedings rather than preservation stage."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["善用Rule 197.3申请审查替代补充专家意见诉求", "将实质性技术抗辩保留至主程序阶段集中发力"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Focus on Rule 197.3 review mechanisms instead of pursuing supplementary expert opinions post-inspection.", "Practice note: Defer substantive technical defenses to main proceedings; use preservation phase strictly for procedural compliance.", "What to monitor: How courts handle technical discrepancies and document accessibility questions raised during preservation in future main actions."]
core_points_en: Request for supplementary expert opinion after ex parte inspection denied due to lack of legal basis.
Distinction made between court-commissioned inspectors and formal court experts under Rule 187 RoP.
Substantive objections to evidence accuracy must be raised in main proceedings rather than preservation stage.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Focus on Rule 197.3 review mechanisms instead of pursuing supplementary expert opinions post-inspection.
Practice note: Defer substantive technical defenses to main proceedings; use preservation phase strictly for procedural compliance.
What to monitor: How courts handle technical discrepancies and document accessibility questions raised during preservation in future main actions.

items.title: Rolls-Royce tops UK’s most iconic trade marks poll
items.title_zh: 劳斯莱斯当选英国最具标志性商标
items.title_en: Rolls-Royce Named UK’s Most Iconic Trademark in UKIPO Centenary Poll
items.summary: Rolls-Royce has been named the UK’s most iconic trade mark in a recent poll conducted by the UK Intellectual Property Office (UK IPO).
items.ai_summary_zh: 为庆祝英国商标注册150周年，英国知识产权局（UKIPO）开展民意调查，评选出塑造公众生活的十大标志性品牌。劳斯莱斯凭借百年工程工艺与品质象征荣登榜首，紧随其后的是Radio Caroline、Twinings和Cadbury等。此次调查旨在回顾商标如何塑造英国的文化与商业历史，目前英国受保护商标超250万件，彰显了其作为质量、信任与身份象征的重要地位。
items.ai_summary_en: To mark the 150th anniversary of the UK trade mark register, the UK Intellectual Property Office conducted a public poll identifying brands that have significantly shaped cultural and commercial life. Rolls-Royce secured the top position, recognized for its enduring legacy of engineering excellence and status as a global symbol of quality. The Radio Caroline broadcasting station placed second, reflecting its profound impact on youth culture and modern radio. Other notable entries in the top ten included Twinings and Cadbury, brands celebrated for their deep heritage and association with consumer trust. This initiative highlights the evolution of the UK trade mark landscape, which now protects over 2.5 million marks. The results underscore how iconic brands serve as powerful symbols of identity and reliability, bridging historical significance with contemporary commercial value in the British market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国品牌出海英国应重视商标的长期品牌建设与文化共鸣，而非仅关注注册数量。借鉴劳斯莱斯等案例，通过持续的品质输出建立“信任”与“身份”认同，有助于提升品牌在英市场的 iconic 地位及抗风险能力。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders operating in the UK, this poll underscores that long-term brand equity relies heavily on cultural resonance and consistent quality rather than mere registration volume. Establishing a brand as an 'icon' requires sustained investment in consumer trust and identity alignment over decades. Companies should view trade marks not just as legal assets but as cultural touchstones. Building this depth of recognition enhances brand resilience against market fluctuations and competitive pressures. Strategic IP management must therefore integrate marketing narratives that emphasize heritage, reliability, and shared consumer experiences to achieve lasting market prominence and defensive strength.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UKIPO举办百五十周年庆典，劳斯莱斯获评英国最具标志性商标", "Radio Caroline、Twinings等品牌入选前十，体现深厚文化积淀", "英国现存超250万注册商标，彰显其在商业与文化中的核心价值"]

items.title: Unified Patent Court Insights: Claim Construction
items.title_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫分院：打印机供墨容器权利要求解释与侵权判定
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf: Functional 'Mountable' Language Defines Product Claim Scope, Not System
items.summary: A recent decision from the Düsseldorf Local Division in UPC_CFI_351/2024 provides an interesting look at the Unified Patent Court's (UPC) evolving approach to claim construction and the role...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）杜塞尔多夫地方法院在UPC_CFI_351/2024案中裁定，描述为“可拆卸安装”的打印机供墨容器属于产品权利要求而非系统权利要求。法院认定“可安装”仅界定产品适用性，不要求外部设备存在。因此，被告单独销售的墨盒构成直接侵权。该判决明确了功能性语言在权利要求解释中的作用，区分了产品与系统权利要求的边界，对后续类似案件的侵权判定具有指导意义。
items.ai_summary_en: In case UPC_CFI_351/2024, the Unified Patent Court’s Düsseldorf Local Division clarified the interpretation of functional language in patent claims. The court ruled that a printer supply container described as 'detachably mountable' constitutes a product claim rather than a system claim. The judgment established that terms like 'mountable' define the product's suitability for intended use, without requiring the physical presence of the external apparatus, such as the printer itself. Consequently, the defendant’s standalone toner bottles were found to directly infringe the patent. This decision highlights the UPC’s evolving approach to distinguishing between product and system claim boundaries, emphasizing that functional limitations do not necessarily expand claim scope to include the entire system environment.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出口企业需警惕单一零部件在UPC被认定为直接侵权的风险。在撰写欧洲专利时，应审慎使用“可安装”等功能性限定，避免将产品权利要求不当扩大至系统层面。建议针对核心组件进行独立权利要求布局，并在出口前进行FTO分析，评估单独销售行为在UPC体系下的侵权可能性，以规避高额赔偿风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling significantly impacts how functional limitations are interpreted in European patent litigation. Parties should recognize that describing a component as 'mountable' or 'capable of supporting' does not automatically convert a product claim into a system claim requiring the full assembly for infringement. For rights holders, this strengthens protection for standalone spare parts and consumables. Conversely, manufacturers of individual components must conduct thorough freedom-to-operate analyses, considering that their products may infringe even when sold separately from the main device. Careful claim drafting is essential to avoid unintended broadening of scope through functional terminology.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院裁定“可安装”界定产品适用性，无需外部设备存在即构成侵权", "明确区分产品权利要求与系统权利要求，单独销售组件可直接侵权", "确立UPC在功能性语言解释上的倾向，强化专利权人对配套产品的保护"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The court ruled that 'mountable' defines product suitability for intended use, not requiring the external apparatus to be present.", "A clear distinction was drawn between product and system claims, establishing that standalone components can constitute direct infringement.", "The decision confirms the UPC's tendency to interpret functional language as defining product capabilities rather than system dependencies."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕单一零部件在UPC被认定为直接侵权的法律风险", "优化欧洲专利权利要求布局，审慎使用功能性限定语言"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Standalone components may face direct infringement liability under product claims using functional language.", "Practice note: Drafters should carefully evaluate functional terms to prevent unintended expansion from product to system claim scope.", "What to monitor: Future UPC decisions regarding the boundary between product suitability definitions and system-level requirements."]
core_points_en: The court ruled that 'mountable' defines product suitability for intended use, not requiring the external apparatus to be present.
A clear distinction was drawn between product and system claims, establishing that standalone components can constitute direct infringement.
The decision confirms the UPC's tendency to interpret functional language as defining product capabilities rather than system dependencies.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Standalone components may face direct infringement liability under product claims using functional language.
Practice note: Drafters should carefully evaluate functional terms to prevent unintended expansion from product to system claim scope.
What to monitor: Future UPC decisions regarding the boundary between product suitability definitions and system-level requirements.

items.title: World Intellectual Property Day 2026: New WIPO Data Show IP Use Booming in Sports Industry
items.title_zh: 2026世界知识产权日：WIPO数据显示体育行业专利使用激增
items.title_en: WIPO Report: Patent Activity Surges in Golf, Swimming, and Racket Sports
items.summary: Golf, swimming and racket sports are the most patent-intensive areas of the sports industry, where use of intellectual property (IP) is rising at above-average rates, according to a new World Intellectual Property Organization report released to mark World IP Day.
items.ai_summary_zh: 为纪念2026年世界知识产权日，世界知识产权组织（WIPO）发布最新报告，揭示体育产业知识产权使用的显著增长趋势。数据显示，高尔夫、游泳和球拍类运动是专利最密集的细分领域，其知识产权使用率以高于平均水平的速度上升。这一动态反映了体育科技与装备创新的热度，表明相关领域的技术壁垒正在快速构建，全球市场竞争正从单纯的品牌营销向核心技术专利布局转移。
items.ai_summary_en: To mark World IP Day 2026, the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) released a new report highlighting significant growth trends in intellectual property utilization within the sports industry. The data reveals that golf, swimming, and racket sports are currently the most patent-intensive segments, with IP usage rising at rates exceeding the industry average. This surge reflects heightened innovation in sports technology and equipment, indicating that technical barriers are being rapidly constructed. Consequently, global market competition is shifting from traditional brand marketing toward strategic core technology patent layouts. These developments underscore the increasing importance of robust IP portfolios in securing competitive advantages and navigating the evolving landscape of sports-related technological advancements.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，国际后续是否会围绕这类数据发布给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: Stakeholders operating in high-growth sports segments should prioritize freedom-to-operate analyses to mitigate infringement risks in patent-dense fields like golf and swimming. Strengthening overseas protection for core materials and designs enhances bargaining power and brand defensibility in European markets. As competition shifts toward technical patents, companies must adapt their strategies to address these emerging barriers effectively.

items.ai_core_points_en: ["WIPO reports above-average growth in intellectual property usage across the broader sports industry sector.", "Golf, swimming, and racket sports are identified as the current core areas with the highest patent density.", "Accelerating technical innovation makes intellectual property a key element for building market competitive advantages."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["聚焦高尔夫等专利密集区，提前开展FTO分析以规避侵权风险", "强化核心技术与设计专利的海外布局，提升欧洲市场竞争力"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Focus on patent-dense sectors such as golf and swimming to proactively conduct FTO analyses and avoid infringement liabilities.", "Practice note: Reinforce overseas layouts for core technologies and design patents to strengthen competitiveness and brand moats in Europe.", "What to monitor: Track the shift from brand-centric marketing to core technology patent strategies as technical barriers rise in the sports industry."]
core_points_en: WIPO reports above-average growth in intellectual property usage across the broader sports industry sector.
Golf, swimming, and racket sports are identified as the current core areas with the highest patent density.
Accelerating technical innovation makes intellectual property a key element for building market competitive advantages.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Focus on patent-dense sectors such as golf and swimming to proactively conduct FTO analyses and avoid infringement liabilities.
Practice note: Reinforce overseas layouts for core technologies and design patents to strengthen competitiveness and brand moats in Europe.
What to monitor: Track the shift from brand-centric marketing to core technology patent strategies as technical barriers rise in the sports industry.

items.title: No hindsight allowed: the UPC rejects inventive step attacks that are based on fragmented analyses (UPC_CFI_337/2025)
items.title_zh: UPC裁定：创造性评估严禁基于碎片化分析的后见之明
items.title_en: UPC Munich Rejects Hindsight: Inventive Step Requires Holistic Prior Art Assessment
items.summary: Court docket: CD Munich
items.ai_summary_zh: 慕尼黑中央分部在TCL诉康宁案中裁定，评估创造性时不得仅因结构相似就孤立选取最接近现有技术的特定实施例作为起点，除非现有技术本身有指引，否则构成后见之明。法院强调必须将现有技术视为整体，且若权利要求特征相互依赖以解决客观问题，禁止将其人为拆分进行孤立显而易见性评估。该判决澄清了UPC与EPO实践的差异，要求从整体技术教导出发，避免碎片化分析导致的逻辑偏差。
items.ai_summary_en: In TCL Europe SAS v. Corning Incorporated, the UPC Munich Central Division clarified inventive step methodology in Order UPC_CFI_337/2025. The Court ruled that selecting a specific prior art embodiment as the starting point solely due to structural similarity constitutes hindsight unless the disclosure itself provides a pointer. Prior art must be assessed as a whole, deriving the starting point from its overall technical teaching rather than isolated examples. Furthermore, the Court prohibited artificially splitting interdependent claim features for isolated obviousness assessments. Where features jointly solve an objective problem, their interdependencies must be respected. This decision highlights a divergence from common EPO practices, emphasizing that fragmented analyses leading to logical biases are impermissible under UPC jurisprudence.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment establishes a stricter holistic standard for inventive step assessment at the UPC compared to traditional EPO approaches. Parties challenging patents must demonstrate clear pointers within prior art when isolating specific embodiments, avoiding fragmented feature-by-feature analysis. Conversely, patent proprietors should emphasize functional dependencies and the integrated nature of claimed solutions to counter such attacks. Litigants must align their arguments with this holistic framework, ensuring that both validity challenges and defenses address the technical teaching as a coherent whole rather than relying on mechanical feature comparisons or artificial segmentation of the invention.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["选取最接近现有技术需有内在指引，孤立选例易构成后见之明", "特征相互依赖时禁止拆分评估，须结合整体技术方案判断", "UPC强调从整体技术教导推导起点，区别于EPO常见实践"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Selecting closest prior art requires internal pointers; isolating examples based on structure risks hindsight bias.", "Interdependent claim features solving an objective problem cannot be split for isolated obviousness assessment.", "UPC mandates deriving the starting point from the prior art's overall technical teaching, differing from EPO practice."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["主张创造性需论证特征依赖与整体协同，避免被拆分攻击", "抗辩时需证明现有技术存在明确指引，防范后见之明指控"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Patentees should strengthen specifications to highlight functional interdependencies, while challengers must identify explicit pointers in prior art to avoid hin…", "Practice note: Avoid fragmented feature analysis; argue from the overall technical teaching of prior art and respect the synergistic or interdependent nature of claim elements.", "What to monitor: Future UPC decisions applying this holistic approach to determine if 'most features in common' remains a valid standalone criterion for selecting the closest prio…"]
core_points_en: Selecting closest prior art requires internal pointers; isolating examples based on structure risks hindsight bias.
Interdependent claim features solving an objective problem cannot be split for isolated obviousness assessment.
UPC mandates deriving the starting point from the prior art's overall technical teaching, differing from EPO practice.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Patentees should strengthen specifications to highlight functional interdependencies, while challengers must identify explicit pointers in prior art to avoid hin…
Practice note: Avoid fragmented feature analysis; argue from the overall technical teaching of prior art and respect the synergistic or interdependent nature of claim elements.
What to monitor: Future UPC decisions applying this holistic approach to determine if 'most features in common' remains a valid standalone criterion for selecting the closest prio…

items.title: [Blog] Innovation en Europe : les femmes toujours sous-représentées selon l’OEB
items.title_zh: EPO报告：欧洲创新中女性发明人代表性持续不足
items.title_en: EPO Report: Women Remain Underrepresented in European Innovation Ecosystem
items.summary: L’Observatoire des brevets et des technologies de l’Office européen des brevets (OEB) a publié, le 3 mars 2026, une étude consacrée à la place des femmes dans l’écosystème européen de l’innovation. Fondée sur une analyse de données relatives aux activités brevet et à l’entrepreneuriat technologique, cette étude met en évidence une sous-représentation persistante des femmes, tant parmi les inventeurs que parmi les fondateurs de start-up impliquées dans des démarches de propriété industrielle.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局（EPO）于2026年3月发布研究，揭示欧洲创新生态中女性长期代表性不足。数据显示，活跃于工业产权的科技初创公司创始人中女性占比不足10%；女性发明人比例虽从2019年的13%微升至2022年的13.8%，但进展缓慢。大学等公共机构的女性发明人比例高于企业。报告指出存在显著的“漏管”现象，即女性在科研和创业路径中逐渐流失，并建议通过改善科学教育准入、消除结构性障碍及加强创业支持来缩小差距。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office (EPO) published a study on March 3, 2026, highlighting the persistent underrepresentation of women within the European innovation ecosystem. Based on an analysis of patent activities and technology entrepreneurship data, the report reveals that fewer than 10% of founders in tech startups actively engaged in industrial property processes are women. The proportion of female inventors shows only modest growth, rising from approximately 13% in 2019 to 13.8% in 2022. Significant disparities exist across sectors and organizational structures, with universities and public research institutions demonstrating higher rates of female inventorship compared to private enterprises. The study identifies a 'leaky pipeline' phenomenon, where women gradually drop out of scientific and technological career paths due to institutional and economic barriers. It recommends improving access to scientific education, removing structural obstacles, and strengthening support for technology entrepreneurship to address these gaps.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类数据发布给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This report underscores the European Union's continued focus on diversity within the innovation landscape. While not a direct legal change, it signals growing institutional emphasis on inclusive practices. Organizations operating in Europe should consider how team composition influences local collaboration opportunities and eligibility for government funding or grants. Prioritizing the retention of female technical talent and optimizing internal promotion mechanisms can align operations with prevailing European social values. Enhancing diversity metrics may improve brand reputation and competitive positioning in markets where corporate social responsibility and equitable innovation practices are increasingly scrutinized by stakeholders and regulatory bodies.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["女性科技初创创始人占比不足10%，发明人比例仅微增至13.8%", "公共研究机构女性发明人比例高于私营企业，存在显著行业差异", "报告识别“漏管”效应，建议改善教育准入及消除职业结构障碍"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Female founders in IP-active tech startups remain below 10%, while female inventor share rose marginally to 13.8% by 2022.", "Public research institutions exhibit higher female inventor proportions than private companies, reflecting significant sectoral differences.", "The report identifies a 'leaky pipeline' effect and recommends enhancing educational access and removing structural career barriers."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重视团队多样性，可能影响当地合作及政府资助申请资格", "优化女性人才保留机制，符合欧洲价值观以提升品牌竞争力"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Team diversity may influence local partnership opportunities and eligibility for specific EU government funding programs.", "Practice note: Optimize female talent retention and promotion mechanisms to align with European social values and enhance brand competitiveness.", "What to monitor: Evolving criteria for diversity and inclusion in future EU innovation grants, public procurement, and collaborative research frameworks."]
core_points_en: Female founders in IP-active tech startups remain below 10%, while female inventor share rose marginally to 13.8% by 2022.
Public research institutions exhibit higher female inventor proportions than private companies, reflecting significant sectoral differences.
The report identifies a 'leaky pipeline' effect and recommends enhancing educational access and removing structural career barriers.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Team diversity may influence local partnership opportunities and eligibility for specific EU government funding programs.
Practice note: Optimize female talent retention and promotion mechanisms to align with European social values and enhance brand competitiveness.
What to monitor: Evolving criteria for diversity and inclusion in future EU innovation grants, public procurement, and collaborative research frameworks.

items.title: UKIPO updates graphic symbols, interfaces and animated designs practice - GJE
items.title_zh: UKIPO更新图形符号、界面及动画外观设计审查实践
items.title_en: UKIPO Issues Practice Notice Updating Registered Design Rules for Graphic Symbols, GUIs, and Animated Interfaces
items.summary: The UKIPO has issued a Designs Practice Notice, updating UK practice on registered designs relating to graphic symbols/icons and GUIs
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局（UKIPO）发布罕见的外观设计实务通知DPN 1/26，更新了关于图形符号、图标、图形用户界面（GUI）及包含动画或动态效果的设计注册审查实践。该通知重点明确了动画GUI和序列设计的审查标准，为申请人和代理人提供了额外指导。尽管具体日常影响尚待观察，但此举旨在澄清审查方法，提升申请确定性。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office has issued a rare Designs Practice Notice, DPN 1/26, significantly updating examination practices for registered designs involving graphic symbols, icons, and graphical user interfaces. This guidance specifically addresses designs incorporating animation or movement, clarifying the standards for animated GUIs and sequence designs. The notice aims to provide additional clarity and guidance for applicants and representatives, enhancing transparency in the registration process. While the update offers valuable direction on how the UKIPO will approach these dynamic design elements, questions remain regarding how this updated practice aligns with global design systems. The practical day-to-day effects on UK design practice are yet to be fully observed, but the move is intended to improve certainty for stakeholders navigating the registration of complex digital interface designs.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国科技企业出海英国时，需密切关注GUI及动态交互界面的外观专利申请细节。建议提前优化设计视图与动态描述，确保符合UKIPO最新审查指引，以降低驳回风险并提升保护范围的有效性。
items.ai_insight_en: Stakeholders seeking protection for digital products in the United Kingdom should carefully review the updated examination criteria for graphical user interfaces and dynamic interaction elements. It is advisable to optimize design representations and detailed descriptions of motion sequences early in the preparation phase. Aligning submissions with the latest UKIPO guidance can help mitigate rejection risks and ensure the validity of the granted protection scope. Practitioners should focus on precise drafting of animated sequences to meet the new procedural expectations, thereby improving the likelihood of successful registration for complex interface designs.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UKIPO发布DPN 1/26通知，明确GUI及动画外观设计的审查新标准", "重点规范含动画或运动效果的图形界面及序列设计的注册要求", "提供额外申请指导以增强审查透明度，但全球协调性仍存疑问"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UKIPO released DPN 1/26 establishing new examination standards for graphical user interfaces and animated design registrations.", "The notice specifically regulates registration requirements for graphic interfaces featuring animation or motion effects.", "Additional application guidance is provided to enhance examination transparency, though global coordination remains uncertain."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["优化GUI及动态设计视图以符合UKIPO最新审查指引", "关注动画序列描述的规范性以提升英国外观授权率"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Optimize GUI and dynamic design views to comply with the latest UKIPO examination guidelines.", "Practice note: Focus on standardizing animated sequence descriptions to improve authorization rates for UK registered designs.", "What to monitor: Observe how the updated practice integrates with international design systems and its daily operational impact."]
core_points_en: UKIPO released DPN 1/26 establishing new examination standards for graphical user interfaces and animated design registrations.
The notice specifically regulates registration requirements for graphic interfaces featuring animation or motion effects.
Additional application guidance is provided to enhance examination transparency, though global coordination remains uncertain.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Optimize GUI and dynamic design views to comply with the latest UKIPO examination guidelines.
Practice note: Focus on standardizing animated sequence descriptions to improve authorization rates for UK registered designs.
What to monitor: Observe how the updated practice integrates with international design systems and its daily operational impact.

items.title: Win some, lose some: UPC upholds Dainese’s airbag patent but finds it not infringed
items.title_zh: UPC 维持 Dainese 气囊专利有效但裁定未侵权
items.title_en: UPC Milan Division Upholds Dainese Airbag Patent Validity but Rules No Infringement by Alpinestars
items.summary: Almost exactly a year after the UPC’s Milan local division asserted its jurisdiction for Spain, it has ruled that the patent-in-suit EP 4 072 364 is valid but not infringed by Alpinestars’ products (case IDs: UPC_CFI_472/2024, UPC CFI 792/2024, UPC CFI 831/2024 and UPC CFI 182/2025). Initially, the parties were fighting over Dainese’s two patents […] The post Win some, lose some: UPC upholds Dainese’s airbag patent but finds it not infringed appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）米兰地方分部在涉及西班牙管辖权的案件中作出裁决，确认 Dainese 拥有的 EP4072364 号气囊专利有效，但认定竞争对手 Alpinestars 的产品未构成侵权。该案历经多起诉讼合并审理，体现了 UPC 在专利确权与侵权判定上的分离裁判思路，对摩托车安全装备领域的专利布局具有参考意义。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Milan Local Division has issued a judgment confirming that European patent EP 4 072 364, owned by Dainese, remains valid. However, the court simultaneously ruled that competing products manufactured by Alpinestars do not infringe upon the patent claims. This decision follows consolidated proceedings across multiple case IDs, reflecting the UPC’s established practice of separating validity assessments from infringement determinations. The ruling underscores the court's jurisdictional reach, having previously asserted authority over Spain. For stakeholders in the motorcycle safety equipment sector, this outcome highlights the strategic importance of precise claim construction and thorough freedom-to-operate analyses when navigating cross-border patent disputes within the new unified system.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: Patent holders should recognize the commercial risk associated with 'valid but not infringed' outcomes, where enforcement costs are incurred without securing market exclusivity. Litigants can leverage the UPC’s centralized mechanism to manage multinational disputes efficiently, potentially reducing overall legal expenditures compared to parallel national actions. However, parties must conduct rigorous infringement comparisons early in the process. Monitoring the stability of patents in specific technical fields is also crucial, as validity challenges remain a key defensive strategy for alleged infringers seeking to neutralize threats without admitting liability.

The court determined that Alpinestars’ accused products fall outside the scope of protection defined by the patent claims.
This case illustrates the UPC’s practical application of jurisdiction across member states while handling consolidated litigation.

Practice note: Utilize the UPC’s single forum to streamline defense strategies and reduce costs in cross-border conflicts.
What to monitor: Track judicial trends regarding claim interpretation and patent stability in high-value technology sectors.

items.title: Paris local division revokes Michelin patent in Goodyear case
items.title_zh: 巴黎地方法院在固特异案中撤销米其林专利
items.title_en: UPC Paris Local Division Revokes Michelin Patent in Goodyear Infringement Dispute
items.summary: Michelin filed an infringement action at the Paris local division in February 2025. The patent was only valid in France at the time of the action. The company argued that its competitor Goodyear infringed its intellectual property rights with the car tyres Fuelmax D Endurance and the Fuelmax D GEN-2, which are offered and placed […] The post Paris local division revokes Michelin patent in Goodyear case appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 米其林于 2025 年 2 月在统一专利法院巴黎地方法院起诉固特异侵权，涉案专利当时仅在法国有效。米其林指控固特异 Fuelmax 系列轮胎侵权。然而，巴黎地方法院最终作出判决，撤销了米其林的该项专利，驳回了其侵权主张。此案凸显了 UPC 地方分院在专利有效性审查上的关键作用及诉讼风险。
items.ai_summary_en: Michelin initiated an infringement action against Goodyear at the Unified Patent Court’s Paris Local Division in February 2025. At the time of filing, the asserted patent held validity solely within France. Michelin alleged that Goodyear infringed its intellectual property rights through the marketing and distribution of specific car tyres, namely the Fuelmax D Endurance and Fuelmax D GEN-2 models. The Paris Local Division ultimately ruled in favor of Goodyear by revoking the contested Michelin patent. Consequently, the court dismissed Michelin’s infringement claims entirely. This judgment underscores the critical role of UPC local divisions in assessing patent validity and highlights the significant litigation risks associated with enforcing patents that lack broader unitary or multi-jurisdictional coverage during proceedings.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，法国后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This case illustrates the strategic importance of challenging patent validity early in UPC proceedings, particularly when the asserted right lacks unitary effect. Defendants should actively utilize counterclaims for revocation to neutralize infringement risks, even against seemingly robust patents. For rights holders, reliance on single-country national patents exposes them to concentrated invalidity attacks that can dismantle entire enforcement strategies. Practitioners must assess the territorial scope of patents before litigation and prepare rigorous evidence-based defenses focused on novelty and inventive step to effectively leverage the UPC’s bifurcated system.

items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage UPC counterclaims to challenge patent validity early, especially against nationally limited rights.", "Practice note: Concentrate resources on invalidity arguments for single-jurisdiction patents to mitigate infringement exposure.", "What to monitor: Track how local divisions handle validity challenges for non-unitary patents in cross-border disputes."]

The asserted patent possessed only national validity in France at the time the infringement action was commenced.

Practice note: Concentrate resources on invalidity arguments for single-jurisdiction patents to mitigate infringement exposure.
What to monitor: Track how local divisions handle validity challenges for non-unitary patents in cross-border disputes.

items.title: UPC: Security for costs not required where claimant is backed by appropriate litigation insurance
items.title_zh: UPC 裁定：适当诉讼保险下无需提供费用担保
items.title_en: UPC Appeal Court: Security for Costs Unjustified Where Litigation Insurance Mitigates Enforcement Risk
items.summary: The UPC Court of Appeal has ruled that an order for security for costs is not justified where a claimant’s litigation insurance removes...
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC 上诉院在 Syntorr 诉 Arthrex 案中推翻慕尼黑分部命令，裁定若原告拥有含“反规避条款”的适当诉讼保险，能消除费用执行风险，则无需依程序规则第 158 条提供费用担保。法院虽未明确保险可正式替代银行保函，但确认评估担保申请时必须考量此类保险，已下令释放原冻结的 200 万欧元担保。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal overturned a Munich Local Division order requiring the claimant to provide €2 million in security for costs under Rule 158 of the Rules of Procedure. In Syntorr v Arthrex, the appellate court determined that the claimant’s existing litigation insurance, which included an anti-avoidance endorsement, effectively eliminated any genuine concern regarding the enforceability of potential adverse costs orders. Consequently, the requirement for a bank guarantee or deposit was deemed unnecessary, and the previously frozen funds were ordered released. While the Court did not formally rule that insurance substitutes for traditional security mechanisms under Rule 158, it established that such coverage must be central to assessing security applications. This decision clarifies that appropriate insurance removing enforcement risks precludes the need for additional financial guarantees.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧发起专利诉讼时，应配置含反规避条款的高质量诉讼保险，以降低资金占用并规避费用担保令。同时需关注 UPC 对财务实力与保险覆盖范围的审查标准，优化诉讼融资结构。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment significantly impacts litigation strategy before the UPC by reducing the financial burden on claimants seeking to enforce patent rights. Parties should ensure their litigation funding arrangements include robust insurance policies with anti-avoidance clauses to mitigate the risk of costly security orders. Legal teams must carefully document how such insurance covers potential adverse costs to satisfy judicial scrutiny regarding enforceability. Monitoring future case law will be essential to understand how broadly the Court applies this principle to different types of insurance products and whether formal substitution rules for Rule 158 evolve. Effective structuring of litigation finance can now lower entry barriers for patent enforcement actions in Europe.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["上诉院认定适当诉讼保险可消除费用无法执行的实质担忧", "推翻慕尼黑分部要求提供 200 万欧元费用担保的原审命令", "明确未来评估费用担保申请时须将诉讼保险纳入核心考量"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Appellate court found that adequate litigation insurance removes legitimate concerns about enforcing adverse costs orders against the claimant.", "Munich Local Division's €2 million security order was set aside, with instructions issued for the immediate release of the provided bank guarantee.", "Future assessments of security for costs applications must centrally consider the scope and validity of the claimant's litigation insurance."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["配置含反规避条款的诉讼保险可有效替代高额现金担保", "利用该判例优化诉讼资金结构，降低在欧维权门槛"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Robust litigation insurance with anti-avoidance features can prevent substantial capital from being tied up in bank guarantees during UPC proceedings.", "Practice note: When opposing security for costs motions, counsel should proactively demonstrate how existing insurance coverage fully addresses the defendant's enforcement concern…", "What to monitor: Future UPC decisions clarifying whether specific insurance policy terms are sufficient to formally substitute for deposits or guarantees under Rule 158."]
core_points_en: Appellate court found that adequate litigation insurance removes legitimate concerns about enforcing adverse costs orders against the claimant.
Munich Local Division's €2 million security order was set aside, with instructions issued for the immediate release of the provided bank guarantee.
Future assessments of security for costs applications must centrally consider the scope and validity of the claimant's litigation insurance.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Robust litigation insurance with anti-avoidance features can prevent substantial capital from being tied up in bank guarantees during UPC proceedings.
Practice note: When opposing security for costs motions, counsel should proactively demonstrate how existing insurance coverage fully addresses the defendant's enforcement concern…
What to monitor: Future UPC decisions clarifying whether specific insurance policy terms are sufficient to formally substitute for deposits or guarantees under Rule 158.

items.title: UPC procedure – Intra-procedural condition permitted
items.title_zh: UPC 程序裁决：允许附条件的无效反诉请求
items.title_en: UPC Munich Division Permits Infringement-Conditional Counterclaim for Revocation
items.summary: In a recent dispute at the Munich Local Division (UPC_CFI_628/2024, UPC_CFI_125/2025), Emboline Inc. initiated infringement proceedings...
items.ai_summary_zh: 慕尼黑地方法院在 Emboline 诉 AorticLab 案中裁定，被告可提出以侵权成立为前提的附条件无效反诉。法院认定该做法不违反 UPC 协定及程序规则，且未损害原告利益。因最终判定不侵权，反诉无需审理，相关费用由被告承担。
items.ai_summary_en: In a recent dispute before the Munich Local Division of the Unified Patent Court (Emboline Inc. v. AorticLab srl), the Court addressed the admissibility of an intra-procedural condition attached to a counterclaim for revocation. The defendant declared that their validity challenge would only proceed if the Court first found patent infringement. The Court ruled that neither the UPC Agreement nor the Rules of Procedure prohibit such conditions, noting that the claimant suffers no disadvantage. Consequently, the counterclaim was deemed limited under Rule 263.3 RoP. Since the Court ultimately found no infringement, it did not rule on the validity counterclaim. However, the costs associated with the conditional counterclaim were deemed unnecessary, and the defendant was ordered to bear these specific costs.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对 UPC 诉讼时，需谨慎评估提起无效反诉的策略与时机。若侵权风险较低，盲目提起附条件反诉可能导致不必要的费用损失，应结合案件实质证据制定精细化诉讼方案。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling clarifies procedural flexibility within the UPC, allowing parties to structure litigation strategies more efficiently. By linking validity challenges to infringement findings, defendants can avoid unnecessary proceedings when infringement risks are low. However, practitioners must carefully assess the likelihood of success; if the primary infringement claim fails, the party proposing the conditional counterclaim faces cost penalties for the wasted procedural steps. This highlights the importance of aligning procedural tactics with substantive evidence to optimize resource allocation and minimize financial exposure in European patent disputes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 允许被告提出附条件无效反诉，即以侵权成立为生效前提", "法院认定此类程序安排符合规则且未对专利权人造成不利影响", "若主诉侵权不成立，附条件反诉无需裁决，费用由提出方承担"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC permits defendants to file counterclaims for revocation conditioned on a prior finding of patent infringement.", "Such procedural arrangements comply with the UPCA and Rules of Procedure, provided they do not disadvantage the claimant.", "If infringement is not established, the conditional counterclaim requires no decision, but the defendant bears its costs."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["评估侵权风险后再决定是否提起附条件无效反诉，避免费用浪费", "利用程序规则灵活性，优化欧洲专利诉讼中的攻防策略与成本控制"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Evaluate infringement risk before filing conditional counterclaims to avoid bearing unnecessary costs if the main claim fails.", "Practice note: Leverage Rule 263.3 RoP to limit counterclaims procedurally, ensuring alignment with case strategy and cost control objectives.", "What to monitor: How other UPC divisions apply this precedent regarding intra-procedural conditions and cost allocations in future cases."]
core_points_en: The UPC permits defendants to file counterclaims for revocation conditioned on a prior finding of patent infringement.
Such procedural arrangements comply with the UPCA and Rules of Procedure, provided they do not disadvantage the claimant.
If infringement is not established, the conditional counterclaim requires no decision, but the defendant bears its costs.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Evaluate infringement risk before filing conditional counterclaims to avoid bearing unnecessary costs if the main claim fails.
Practice note: Leverage Rule 263.3 RoP to limit counterclaims procedurally, ensuring alignment with case strategy and cost control objectives.
What to monitor: How other UPC divisions apply this precedent regarding intra-procedural conditions and cost allocations in future cases.

items.title: Court Of Appeal, April 16, 2026, Order, UPC_CoA_54/2026
items.title_zh: UPC上诉法院裁定：上诉期自一审附理由判决作出之日起算
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal: Appeal Period Starts Only Upon Issuance of Reasoned First Instance Decision
items.summary: The Court of Appeal of the Unified Patent Court addresses procedural matters concerning time limits in an appeal case between Belkin International Inc., Belkin B.V., Belkin Limited and Koninklijke Philips N.V. The case involves European Patent EP 2 867 997 and concerns the application of Article 77(1) of the UPCA and various Rules of Procedure.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉法院在Belkin诉飞利浦案中发布程序令，澄清《统一专利法院协议》第77(1)条及程序规则。法院裁定，上诉期限仅在一审法院发布附理由的判决书后才开始计算。理由是上诉人需依据判决理由根据程序规则制定救济请求。该裁决明确了上诉时效的起算节点，解决了此前关于期限计算的争议，为UPC诉讼程序提供了明确的法律确定性。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) Court of Appeal issued a procedural order in the dispute between Belkin and Philips, clarifying the calculation of appeal time limits under Article 77(1) of the UPC Agreement and relevant Rules of Procedure. The Court ruled that the period for lodging an appeal commences only after the Court of First Instance issues a fully reasoned decision. This determination resolves prior ambiguity regarding when the countdown begins. The Court emphasized that the grounds of the decision are indispensable for appellants to properly formulate their remedies pursuant to Rule 225(e) RoP. Consequently, the availability of the reasoned judgment is the definitive trigger for the appeal timeline, providing essential legal certainty for litigants navigating UPC appellate procedures and ensuring parties have sufficient basis to prepare their appeals.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC诉讼时，需严格监控一审判决送达与理由公布的时间差。上诉倒计时并非从判决主文下达即刻启动，而是以完整理由书为准。建议建立专门流程跟踪UPC案件文书状态，避免因误判期限导致失权，同时利用此窗口期充分准备上诉策略。
items.ai_insight_en: Litigants before the Unified Patent Court must distinguish between the delivery of the operative part of a judgment and the subsequent publication of its full reasoning. The appeal clock does not start immediately upon the initial verdict but waits for the complete reasoned decision. Legal teams should implement robust monitoring systems to track the exact date of the reasoned judgment's availability. Relying solely on the initial order risks miscalculating deadlines and losing appeal rights. Parties should utilize the interval between the operative decision and the reasoned judgment to strategically prepare appeal arguments, ensuring compliance with strict procedural timelines while maximizing preparation time.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["上诉期限仅在一审法院发布附理由判决后开始计算", "判决理由对上诉人制定救济请求不可或缺", "明确适用UPCA第77(1)条及程序规则相关条款"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The appeal period commences exclusively upon the issuance of a reasoned decision by the Court of First Instance.", "Reasoned grounds are deemed indispensable for appellants to formulate appropriate remedies under procedural rules.", "The ruling strictly applies Article 77(1) UPCA alongside specific Rules of Procedure provisions."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["严格监控UPC一审判决理由书发布时间以确定上诉期", "利用期限起算规则优化上诉策略并避免程序失权"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure appeal deadlines are calculated from the reasoned judgment date, not the initial verdict, to prevent procedural default.", "Practice note: Establish internal workflows to track the specific release date of full reasoned decisions in UPC cases.", "What to monitor: The gap between the operative part of the judgment and the publication of reasons to optimize appeal strategy timing."]
core_points_en: The appeal period commences exclusively upon the issuance of a reasoned decision by the Court of First Instance.
Reasoned grounds are deemed indispensable for appellants to formulate appropriate remedies under procedural rules.
The ruling strictly applies Article 77(1) UPCA alongside specific Rules of Procedure provisions.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure appeal deadlines are calculated from the reasoned judgment date, not the initial verdict, to prevent procedural default.
Practice note: Establish internal workflows to track the specific release date of full reasoned decisions in UPC cases.
What to monitor: The gap between the operative part of the judgment and the publication of reasons to optimize appeal strategy timing.
